Title: Governance Support Specialist (NAM Programme)
Description:
Primary Location

Belgium-Brussels

NATO Body

NATO International Staff (NATO IS)

Schedule

Full-time

Application Deadline

27-Jul-2026, 12:59:00 AM

Salary (Pay Basis)

5,393.24

Grade

NATO Grade G12


SUMMARY The NATO-Wide Coordination (NWC) Office is an independent office that supports enhanced governance, coherence, and institutional alignment across NATO agencies and bodies. The independent Office reports to the Secretary General through the Director General for Management. NWC serves as the central point of coordination between NATO Headquarters and the governance structures of NATO’s executive bodies, ensuring integrated support to Agency Boards and alignment with NATO’s strategic priorities. The incumbent supports the NATO Airlift Management (NAM) Programme and the Strategic Airlift Capability (SAC) Boards and Committees and is responsible for NATO Support and Procurement Organisation (NSPO) and specifically SAC/NAM governance matters. They perform administrative tasks in support of the Boards and their subordinate bodies. The post is located in Capellen, Luxembourg.
QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE The incumbent must:
have a general education at least to secondary level;
have at least 6 years of experience in administration/secretariat duties;
possess excellent written and verbal communication skills;
demonstrate a sound judgement when communicating with personnel at all levels, including senior military and civilian officials; and
possess the following minimum levels of NATO’s official languages (English/French): V (“Advanced”) in one; I (“Beginner”) in the other.



The following would be considered an advantage:


experience providing governance and administrative support within an international and multicultural organisation;
knowledge of Microsoft SharePoint administration; and
experience supporting budget preparation and monitoring.
MAIN ACCOUNTABILITIES



Planning and Execution: Provide context analysis to the Senior Governance Officer (NAM Programme) on SAC/NAM governance matters. Assist in developing guidelines for the format, accuracy and quality of documents submitted to the SAC Steering Board (SB), the NAM Programme Board (PB) and their subordinate bodies. Organise and support SAC SB/NAM PB meetings and related social events. Arrange travel and meeting schedules. Serve as Secretary of the SAC Personnel Conference in the absence of the Senior Governance Officer. Deputise secretariat functions as required.

Information Management: Ensure timely and accurate input, distribution and follow-up of all documents related to the SAC SB, NAM PB and their subordinate bodies. Produce and maintain tools to track the implementation of SAC SB/NAM PB decisions. Maintain effective follow-up mechanisms for correspondence and documentation. Support preparation of standardized documentation and maintain the governance document index.

Stakeholder Management: Assist the Senior Governance Officer in liaising with key stakeholders of the SAC/NAM Programme, including Chairpersons, Nations, senior management from the NAM Programme Office and the Heavy Airlift Wing. Respond to queries from SAC/NAM national representatives and NATO Support and Procurement Agency (NSPA) staff to ensure procedures are observed. Liaise with NSPA and Programme Office personnel to ensure document consistency, accuracy and timely provision.

Financial Management: Monitor NSPO Secretariat expenditures related to NAM Programme activities and work in close coordination with the NSPO Budget Fund Manager on the administration of the NAM-related budget.

Perform any other related duty as assigned.


INTERRELATIONSHIPS Reporting Line: The incumbent reports to the Senior Governance Officer (NAM Programme). Interactions: The incumbent interacts regularly with NAM Programme Board Chairpersons, national representatives, NSPA NAM Programme Office staff, the NSPO Secretariat, the NSPO Budget Fund Manager, and relevant NSPA and International Staff Divisions and Independent Offices. Direct reports: N/A Indirect reports: N/A
COMPETENCIES The incumbent must demonstrate:
Clarity and Accuracy: monitors others' work for clarity.
Customer Service Orientation: takes personal responsibility for correcting problems.
Empathy: reads non-verbal cues and understands meanings.
Flexibility: adapts to unforeseen situations.
Initiative: is decisive in a time-sensitive situation.
Organisational Awareness: understands organisational climate and culture.
Teamwork: solicits inputs and encourages others.
CONTRACT



Contract to be offered to the successful applicant (if non-seconded): Definite duration contract of three years’ duration; possibility of an indefinite duration contract thereafter.

Contract clause applicable:

In accordance with the NATO Civilian Personnel Regulations, the successful candidate will receive a definite duration contract of three years, which may be followed by an indefinite duration contract.

If the successful applicant is seconded from the national administration of one of NATO’s member States, a 3-year definite duration contract will be offered, which may be renewed for a further period of up to 3 years subject to the agreement of the national authority concerned. The maximum period of service in the post as a seconded staff member is six years.

Serving staff will be offered a contract in accordance with the NATO Civilian Personnel Regulations.


USEFUL INFORMATION REGARDING APPLICATION AND RECRUITMENT PROCESS

Title: Data Consultant (Analyst & Project Manager) - Financial Services
Description:
Data Consultant (Analyst & Project Manager) - Financial Services (min 1 year of experience)




Square Management Belgium est à la recherche de consultants expérimentés (minimum 1 an) possédant une expérience significative et un intérêt dans le domaine de la data dans le secteur financier (banque et assurance).




🏢 Qui est Square Management Belgium ?

Cabinet de référence dans le secteur financier, nous accompagnons nos clients dans leurs projets de transformation grâce à l’expertise de nos 100 consultants.

Square Management Belgium fait partie du groupe Square Management, un cabinet de conseil européen en stratégie et organisation fondé en 2008, présent en France, en Belgique et au Luxembourg.




💡 Qu’attendons-nous de vous dans notre domaine d’expertise Data ?

Identifier et hiérarchiser les actifs de données critiques
Élaborer et maintenir des procédures et des politiques de planification, de surveillance et de contrôle des données
Prendre des décisions basées sur les données grâce à des analyses, des visualisations, des quality checks et des data sets
Structurer et exploiter les données de nos clients pour améliorer et renforcer leur activité
Formuler des recommandations et des conclusions

En interne, vous serez impliqué dans la production de contenus d’expertise (articles, publications, podcasts, …), ainsi que dans la participation aux réponses aux appels d’offres et aux initiatives autour de votre communauté.




🔝 Êtes-vous la personne idéale pour ce poste ?

Vous êtes titulaire d'un Master d'une école de commerce, d'une école d'ingénieur ou d'une université
Vous disposez d'au minimum 1 an d'expérience dans un cabinet de conseil, un environnement projet ou dans les services financiers (banque/assurance)
Vous parlez français ou néerlandais (langue maternelle) et parlez couramment l'anglais
Vous possédez des connaissances et des compétences en data analyse et data management
Des expériences en tant que chef de projet, Business/ Functional Analyst et Process Analyst sont un atout majeur.
Vous êtes orientés client, vous comprenez les besoins et les attentes des parties prenantes
Rigoureux dans l'atteinte de vos objectifs, vous possédez de bonnes capacités d'analyse et de communication orale et écrite
Vous êtes proactif, impliqué et vous adaptez rapidement aux nouvelles situations




🎁 Qu’est-ce que Square Management Belgium peut vous offrir ?

Un parcours professionnel en adéquation avec vos ambitions et votre expérience. Vos responsabilités varient selon votre grade (de consultant confirmé à Principal)
Un parcours de formations articulé autour de nos domaines d'excellence : plus de 150 formations certifiantes (méthodologie, métier, langues, développement personnel)
Une excellente opportunité d’évolution personnelle et professionnelle : des missions enrichissantes pour des clients majeurs dans de multiples secteurs (finance, assurance, banque, secteur public, énergie, etc.) ; partager vos connaissances avec vos collègues et impliquez-vous dans un rôle interne qui encourage l'intrapreneuriat et l'intelligence collective autour de la production de contenus (articles, publications, podcast)
Un environnement de travail où l'équilibre entre vie professionnelle et vie privée, l'ambiance bienveillante et la communication ouverte sont essentiels
Une offre attractive et compétitive : voiture et carte essence, ordinateur, téléphone portable, chèques repas, 13ème mois, programmes de formations, éco-chèques...

  • Vous disposez d'au minimum 1 an d'expérience dans un cabinet de conseil, un environnement projet ou dans les services financiers (banque/assurance)
  • Vous parlez français ou néerlandais (langue maternelle) et parlez couramment l'anglais
  • Vous possédez des connaissances et des compétences en data analyse et data management
  • Des expériences en tant que chef de projet, Business/ Functional Analyst et Process Analyst sont un atout majeur.
  • Vous êtes orientés client, vous comprenez les besoins et les attentes des parties prenantes
  • Rigoureux dans l'atteinte de vos objectifs, vous possédez de bonnes capacités d'analyse et de communication orale et écrite
  • Vous êtes proactif, impliqué et vous adaptez rapidement aux nouvelles situations


🎁 Qu’est-ce que Square Management Belgium peut vous offrir ?

  • Un parcours professionnel en adéquation avec vos ambitions et votre expérience. Vos responsabilités varient selon votre grade (de consultant confirmé à Principal)
  • Un parcours de formations articulé autour de nos domaines d'excellence : plus de 150 formations certifiantes (méthodologie, métier, langues, développement personnel)
  • Une excellente opportunité d’évolution personnelle et professionnelle : des missions enrichissantes pour des clients majeurs dans de multiples secteurs (finance, assurance, banque, secteur public, énergie, etc.) ; partager vos connaissances avec vos collègues et impliquez-vous dans un rôle interne qui encourage l'intrapreneuriat et l'intelligence collective autour de la production de contenus (articles, publications, podcast)
  • Un environnement de travail où l'équilibre entre vie professionnelle et vie privée, l'ambiance bienveillante et la communication ouverte sont essentiels
  • Une offre attractive et compétitive : voiture et carte essence, ordinateur, téléphone portable, chèques repas, 13ème mois, programmes de formations, éco-chèques...

Title: Junior Performance Management Analyst
Description:
Eurofins is an international network of independent life sciences companies, providing a unique range of analytical testing services to clients across multiple industries, to make life and our environment safer, healthier and more sustainable. From the food you eat, to the water you drink, to the medicines you rely on, Eurofins laboratories work with the biggest companies in the world to ensure the products they supply are safe, their ingredients are authentic, and labelling is accurate.



The Eurofins network is dedicated to delivering testing services that contribute to the health and safety of society and the planet, and to its corporate responsibility to protect the environment and ensure diversity, equity, and inclusion across its companies around the world.





Job Description



Are you passionate about turning data into actionable insights and performance improvement? Do you enjoy projects that combine analytics, process optimisation, and stakeholder collaboration? If so, this could be your next role.



We are looking for a Junior Performance Management Analyst to join the team at our corporate office in Brussels, Belgium.



In this role, you will help define and monitor performance across our corporate offices. Your work will have a direct impact on further improving the services that support Eurofins laboratories worldwide.



Responsibilities



As part of our team, you will work on continuous improvement projects to advance performance excellence across the organisation. You will:



Define performance measurement processes.



Help define and implement KPIs that measure the cost efficiency, quality, and timeliness of corporate offices.



Design reporting solutions that provide stakeholders with clear visibility on performance and business outcomes.



Establish audit processes to strengthen governance and operational effectiveness.



Leverage data and process analysis to identify trends, generate actionable insights, and uncover opportunities for improvement.



Conduct root cause analyses to address performance challenges and support sustainable corrective actions.



Partner closely with IT and digital transformation teams to develop and enhance tools, dashboards, and data-driven solutions.



Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders across the organisation to drive improvements and support business transformation initiatives.



What’s in for you?



A unique opportunity to gain experience within a fast‑growing, international organisation.
Exposure to a wide range of corporate departments, providing a comprehensive understanding of how a global organisation operates.

You will join an international office ideally located near parks and green spaces, bringing together colleagues from over 35 nationalities. Additionally, we offer an attractive compensation package and extra-legal benefits that include covered transportation costs, organic meals, sports activities, and annual office events.



Location: Brussels, Belgium. Curious about where you’ll be working? Tour the office: Meet the office - Brussels, Belgium.





Qualifications



Education: Recently graduated with a master’s degree from a top science, engineering or management university.



Language Proficiency: Fluent in English (oral and written). Additional languages are a plus.



Technical Skills: Strong proficiency in MS Office, especially with Excel. Power BI is desirable. Proficiency in data analysis, data visualisation, and reporting tools.



Personal Skills: Strong problem-solver with “can-do” attitude, strong analytical skills, curious and detail-oriented, conceptual thinking skills.





Additional Information



We support your development! Do you feel like you don’t match 100% of the requirements? Don’t hesitate to apply anyway! Eurofins companies are committed to supporting your career development.



We embrace diversity!  As an Equal Opportunity Employer, the Eurofins network of companies believes in strength and innovation through diversity. We prohibit discrimination against employees or applications based on gender identity and/or expression, race, nationality, age, religion, sexual orientation, disability, and everything else that makes employees of Eurofins companies unique.



Find out more on our Careers page: https://careers.eurofins.com/



Company description: Eurofins Scientific is an international network of independent life sciences companies, providing a unique range of analytical testing services to clients across multiple industries, to make life and our environment safer, healthier and more sustainable. From the food you eat, to the water you drink, to the medicines you rely on, Eurofins laboratories work with the biggest companies in the world to ensure the products they supply are safe, their ingredients are authentic and labelling is accurate.

The Eurofins network of companies believes that it is a global leader in food, environment, pharmaceutical and cosmetic product testing and in discovery pharmacology, forensics, advanced material sciences and agroscience contract research services. It is also one of the market leaders in certain testing and laboratory services for genomics, and in the support of clinical studies, as well as in biopharma contract development and manufacturing. It also has a rapidly developing presence in highly specialised and molecular clinical diagnostic testing and in-vitro diagnostic products.



In under 40 years, Eurofins has grown from one laboratory in Nantes, France to over 65,000 employees across a decentralised and entrepreneurial network of over 950 laboratories in over 1,000 independent companies across 59 countries. Eurofins companies offer a portfolio of over 200,000 analytical methods to evaluate the safety, identity, composition, authenticity, origin, traceability and purity of biological substances and products.



The Eurofins network generated EUR 7.296 billion revenues in FY 2025, and has been one of the fastest growing listed European companies for almost three decades.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
At Uni Systems, we are working towards turning digital visions into reality. We are continuously growing and we are looking for a Business Analyst to join our UniQue team.

What will you be doing in this role?


Analyse and structure business needs and translate them into functional and solution requirements
Support business units in identifying appropriate digital solutions in line with the principles of reuse, corporate services, and digital-ready policymaking
Assess and compare solution options (corporate services, existing systems, cloud or low-code solutions) and provide recommendations based on functional, technical, security, and governance considerations
Contribute to IT governance processes, including preparation of documentation and inputs
Monitor technology trends and assess their potential relevance
Support the identification, analysis, and prioritisation of AI use cases, including the assessment of feasibility, risks, value, and alignment with corporate guidance and regulatory requirements
Liaise with technical experts, service providers, and corporate services to support the evaluation and implementation of AI and innovative digital solutions
Support the rollout, optimisation, and adoption of Microsoft 365 collaboration tools


Requirements

What will you be bringing to the team?


Bachelor degree in IT or related field and 11 years of professional experience in IT
Good knowledge of business analysis methods, requirements gathering, and functional specification
Good knowledge of digital solution analysis and selection in a corporate IT environment
Good knowledge of Artificial Intelligence concepts, typical use cases, and implementation approaches
Strong experience in business analysis and stakeholder coordination is essential.
Understanding of IT governance principles, including reuse of corporate solutions and digital-ready policymaking
Knowledge of Artificial Intelligence and Microsoft 365 is expected primarily in an analysis, governance, or adoption context
Digital solution analysis and technology assessment
Microsoft 365 / collaboration and low-code solutions (e.g. Teams, SharePoint, Power Platform)
Certifications or formal training in business analysis, digital transformation, Microsoft 365/Power Platform, Artificial Intelligence, or project management methodologies (e.g. PM², PRINCE2, PMP or equivalent) are considered an asset and will be taken into account during evaluation
Fluent in English at least at a level C1


At Uni Systems, we are providing equal employment opportunities and banning any form of discrimination on grounds of gender, religion, race, color, nationality, disability, social class, political beliefs, age, marital status, sexual orientation or any other characteristics. Take a look at our Diversity, Equality & Inclusion Policy for more information.

Title: Senior Consultant in Data science
Description:
Sia is a next-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.





Job Description



As a Senior consultant in Data Science, you will be involved in transformation projects at our clients. You will execute project assignments in modelling and digital transformation (business/IT analysis and partial coding) environments with a good deal of business perspectives:



Machine learning, AI and statistics: developing predictive models of decision making (supervised and unsupervised learning algorithms, econometrics, forecasting, quantitative diagnosis,...)
Operational research : assistance in the design and implementation of constraint optimization solutions and simulation models
Big Data : Algorithmic knowledge for large and unstructured data processing

You will get the opportunity to actively contribute to Sia’s internal activities:



Development and reinforcement of our offer, by understanding and interpreting data science innovation and challenges
Business development, by contributing to need definition and participating in our commercial initiatives

As fast as possible, you will take up team leadership roles and connect with colleagues of all levels.



Equipped with the entrepreneurial fibre, you are ready to cooperate in a fast growing company.







In return, you can expect:



An international company that gives importance to diversity, inclusion, employee well-being and corporate social responsibilities actions.
A great working atmosphere with friendly and professional colleagues where innovation and entrepreneurship are encouraged
A personal development plan with a regular follow-up as well training opportunities to develop yourself
A competitive salary with great advantages, like mobility budget, hospital insurance, performance -related bonus,...


Qualifications


University Master Civil Engineer (applied mathematics, IT or similar)
2 to 6 years’ experience as a consultant
Outstanding team player with great interpersonal and communication skills
Strong interest in data science and related sectors such as Energy and Finance
You have a strong interest in data and AI-related topics, the ability to quickly build expertise in these areas, and you’re excited about helping our clients seize the opportunities they offer.
Analytical mind, exploring options and able to synthesize into conclusions
Dynamic, result oriented, rigorous and committed
Trilingual: French, Dutch and English
Prepared to participate in temporary assignments abroad


Additional Information



We are unable to provide a work permit for this position



Sia is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or discipline, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, or business needs.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
About TCS:

Tata Consultancy Services is an IT services, consulting and business solutions organization that has been partnering with many of the world’s largest businesses in their transformation journeys for over 50 years. TCS offers a consulting-led, cognitive powered, integrated portfolio of business, technology and engineering services and solutions. This is delivered through its unique Location Independent Agile™ delivery model, recognized as a benchmark of excellence in software development.




TCS has been serving customers in Switzerland since 1985. The company’s presence in Switzerland consists of offices in Zurich, Basel and Nyon. TCS Switzerland offers a broad spectrum of IT Services, Consulting and Financial Solutions. TCS has over 600+ consultants working in Switzerland and over 11000 associates supporting Swiss Clients from Offshore.




About this role:

Serve as a strategic advisor to client stakeholders and technical teams.
Drive business development by identifying and pursuing opportunities to sell data analytics services to Chief Data Officers and other key decision-makers.
Develop and present compelling presentations to senior customer executives and stakeholders
Work with customer to drive workshops and create data analytics charters and business cases.
Demand Creation: Work with customers to generate data analytics project opportunities
Customer Advisory: Lead Data & Analytics Assessment, Strategy and Roadmap engagements and drive customer workshops for analytics use cases
PreSales Leadership: Own stages of the presales cycle, RFI, RFP, and contribute to the development of solutions for presales




Desired Competencies:

Proven experience in a leadership role within data analytics.
Extensive experience with cloud data platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP, Snowflake, Databricks) and Lakehouse architecture.
Demonstrated ability to connect with customer business stakeholders to articulate business problem and approach for data analytics solutions.
Deep understanding of entire data life cycle including ingestion, modelling, data quality, data governance,Analytics solutions
Strong presentation and communication skills, with the ability to engage senior-level audiences.
Able to work with various levels of business organization to coordinate between stakeholders on pre-sales activities
Analyzing, understanding and documenting the business processes in detail where required to guide use case development.
Experience in manufacturing Industry
Strong ability to plan and manage multiple initiatives simultaneously.




Does that sound interesting?




You are welcome to submit your application consisting of your CV, personal letter, relevant degrees or certifications details, professional references, etc. We work with ongoing recruitment so send in your application as soon as possible.




We look forward to receiving your application via LinkedIn or mail to pallavi.malhotra@tcs.com




https://www.tcs.com/who-we-are/legal/tcs-privacy-notice/tcs-privacy-notice-european-union

Title: Middle+ Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Middle+ Marketing Data Analyst based in Switzerland.

This role offers an exciting opportunity to work at the intersection of marketing, analytics, and business intelligence, helping organizations unlock the full value of their marketing data. You will take ownership of end-to-end client delivery, transforming complex datasets into actionable insights that drive strategic decision-making and measurable business outcomes. Working in a fast-paced, remote-first environment, you will collaborate with clients and cross-functional teams to design reporting solutions, ensure data quality, and optimize analytics workflows. The position is ideal for a detail-oriented professional who enjoys solving complex data challenges, communicating insights clearly, and leveraging modern BI, data modeling, and AI technologies to improve performance and efficiency.

Accountabilities


Manage end-to-end analytics delivery for assigned client accounts, ensuring reporting solutions align with business objectives and marketing goals.
Design, build, maintain, and optimize dashboards and reports across business intelligence platforms such as Looker, Power BI, and Tableau.
Develop, troubleshoot, and enhance SQL queries to support analysis, reporting, investigations, and data validation activities.
Create and maintain data transformation workflows, leveraging data modeling best practices and tools such as dbt.
Conduct quality assurance and user acceptance testing to ensure data accuracy, consistency, reliability, and trustworthiness.
Collaborate directly with stakeholders to gather requirements, translate business needs into analytical solutions, and communicate insights effectively.
Identify trends, risks, and opportunities within marketing data and proactively provide recommendations to improve performance.
Utilize AI-powered tools to accelerate analysis, reporting, documentation, and overall productivity.


Requirements


3+ years of experience working as a Data Analyst with a strong focus on marketing analytics and marketing data ecosystems.
Advanced expertise in at least one major BI platform, such as Looker, Power BI, or Tableau, with the ability to work across multiple reporting environments.
Strong SQL skills with experience building, optimizing, and troubleshooting complex queries.
Experience with data modeling methodologies and transformation tools, preferably dbt.
Advanced understanding of Google Analytics and marketing performance measurement principles.
Hands-on experience using Git for version control and collaborative development workflows.
Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving abilities, and exceptional attention to detail.
Proven ability to work directly with clients, communicate technical concepts clearly, and deliver business-focused insights.
Experience performing QA/UAT testing and validating large-scale data sets.
Professional-level English communication skills, both written and verbal.
Familiarity with AI tools and a demonstrated ability to integrate them into daily analytical workflows.
Experience with digital marketing platforms, marketing data sources, or agency environments is considered an advantage.


Benefits


Fully remote work environment with flexibility and autonomy.
Competitive compensation package.
Stock option opportunities.
Comprehensive medical and dental coverage.
Retirement savings support through a 401(k) plan.
Unlimited paid time off policy.
13 paid holidays annually.
Professional development and learning reimbursement programs.
Opportunity to work with innovative AI-powered analytics technologies.
Exposure to global clients and large-scale marketing data projects.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Middle+ Marketing Data Analyst based in Switzerland.

This role offers an exciting opportunity to work at the intersection of marketing, analytics, and business intelligence, helping organizations unlock the full value of their marketing data. You will take ownership of end-to-end client delivery, transforming complex datasets into actionable insights that drive strategic decision-making and measurable business outcomes. Working in a fast-paced, remote-first environment, you will collaborate with clients and cross-functional teams to design reporting solutions, ensure data quality, and optimize analytics workflows. The position is ideal for a detail-oriented professional who enjoys solving complex data challenges, communicating insights clearly, and leveraging modern BI, data modeling, and AI technologies to improve performance and efficiency.

Accountabilities

  • Manage end-to-end analytics delivery for assigned client accounts, ensuring reporting solutions align with business objectives and marketing goals.
  • Design, build, maintain, and optimize dashboards and reports across business intelligence platforms such as Looker, Power BI, and Tableau.
  • Develop, troubleshoot, and enhance SQL queries to support analysis, reporting, investigations, and data validation activities.
  • Create and maintain data transformation workflows, leveraging data modeling best practices and tools such as dbt.
  • Conduct quality assurance and user acceptance testing to ensure data accuracy, consistency, reliability, and trustworthiness.
  • Collaborate directly with stakeholders to gather requirements, translate business needs into analytical solutions, and communicate insights effectively.
  • Identify trends, risks, and opportunities within marketing data and proactively provide recommendations to improve performance.
  • Utilize AI-powered tools to accelerate analysis, reporting, documentation, and overall productivity.

Requirements

  • 3+ years of experience working as a Data Analyst with a strong focus on marketing analytics and marketing data ecosystems.
  • Advanced expertise in at least one major BI platform, such as Looker, Power BI, or Tableau, with the ability to work across multiple reporting environments.
  • Strong SQL skills with experience building, optimizing, and troubleshooting complex queries.
  • Experience with data modeling methodologies and transformation tools, preferably dbt.
  • Advanced understanding of Google Analytics and marketing performance measurement principles.
  • Hands-on experience using Git for version control and collaborative development workflows.
  • Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving abilities, and exceptional attention to detail.
  • Proven ability to work directly with clients, communicate technical concepts clearly, and deliver business-focused insights.
  • Experience performing QA/UAT testing and validating large-scale data sets.
  • Professional-level English communication skills, both written and verbal.
  • Familiarity with AI tools and a demonstrated ability to integrate them into daily analytical workflows.
  • Experience with digital marketing platforms, marketing data sources, or agency environments is considered an advantage.

Benefits

  • Fully remote work environment with flexibility and autonomy.
  • Competitive compensation package.
  • Stock option opportunities.
  • Comprehensive medical and dental coverage.
  • Retirement savings support through a 401(k) plan.
  • Unlimited paid time off policy.
  • 13 paid holidays annually.
  • Professional development and learning reimbursement programs.
  • Opportunity to work with innovative AI-powered analytics technologies.
  • Exposure to global clients and large-scale marketing data projects.

Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

Title: Stagiaire Data Analyst
Description:
Description de l'entreprise

Le présent et le futur d’Audemars Piguet se construisent grâce à la contribution de tous nos talents. Inspirés par la richesse de notre passé, nous sommes enthousiastes à l’idée de ce que nous créerons dans le futur. Ensemble, nous sommes résolument tournés vers l’avenir et en quête continue d’excellence dans tous nos métiers.

Si notre mission vous inspire, venez poursuivre votre parcours au sein de notre famille pour que l’on continue de créer l'extraordinaire. Écrivons le prochain chapitre de votre carrière ensemble !

Description du poste

Dans le cadre d’un projet stratégique de digitalisation de nos ateliers de production, nous proposons un stage de 6 mois, à partir de septembre 2026, centré sur la collecte et l’exploitation des données issues des moyens de mesure utilisés dans le flux de production horloger. Le projet s’inscrit au cœur de problématiques industrielles concrètes et aura un impact direct sur la robustesse et la performance de nos processus.

Dans ce cadre, vous serez en charge des principales tâches suivantes :


Structurer et consolider les données provenant de nos ateliers de production
Développer des outils analytiques permettant de créer de la valeur et de sécuriser notre production
Mettre en application ces outils au sein de notre production afin de proposer des réglages initiaux optimisés


Qualifications


Étudiant·e de Master en Data Science, Microtechnique, Génie mécanique, Informatique, ou domaine apparenté
Connaissances approfondies en programmation (Python ou MATLAB)
Maîtrise de MS office
Intérêt pour les systèmes de mesure et/ou la mécanique horlogère
Capacité à travailler de manière rigoureuse, structurée et méthodique
Esprit d’équipe, sens du terrain et motivation pour interagir de manière transverse

Title: Business & Customer Data Analyst
Description:
Business & Customer Data Analyst

Basel-Stadt




MCH Exhibitions & Events organizes leading B2B and B2C events in Switzerland and manages venue operations at Messe Basel and Messe Zürich.

To strengthen our data-driven decision making, we are looking for a Business & Customer Data Analyst who will transform data into actionable insights across marketing, sales, and event operations.

The role will help unlock the value of our visitor, exhibitor and marketing data to support audience growth, exhibitor success, and strategic decision making across our event portfolio. This position sits within the Technology, Intelligence & Marketing team and works closely with marketing, sales, and event teams.




Your responsibilities

Develop and maintain automated dashboards and reports providing insights on event performance, visitor behaviour, marketing effectiveness, campaign performance, exhibitor value, and business performance
Analyze and consolidate data across CRM, ticketing, marketing platforms, and other systems to generate clear insights and recommendations for business teams and leadership
Support leadership with data-driven reporting for strategic decisions
Develop visitor segmentation, audience intelligence models, and customer insights
Analyze visitor acquisition performance across channels and identify opportunities to improve audience targeting and marketing ROI
Provide insights on visitor behaviour across events, including visitor profiles, engagement, markets, and audiences
Provide analytics supporting exhibitor sales, retention, and value propositions
Support sales teams with data-driven insights to demonstrate exhibitor value
Ensure data quality, consistency, and governance within CRM and related systems
Support and improve CRM data structure, reporting capabilities, and analytics readiness
Define best practices for data management across teams
Support ongoing improvement of data infrastructure, integration, and analytics capabilities
Develop a company-wide event performance dashboard
Build visitor segmentation and audience intelligence models
Support marketing teams with campaign performance analytics
Provide insights to improve exhibitor value propositions
Improve CRM data structure to support analytics




Your profile

3 to 5 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or CRM analytics
Experience working with customer data, marketing data, and CRM analytics, ideally Salesforce
Experience translating complex data into clear business insights for non-technical stakeholders
Strong SQL skills
Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar
Advanced Excel skills
Comfortable using AI tools for building, analysis, and automation
German native and business-proficient English
Nice to have: marketing analytics, data modeling, and experience working with multiple data sources
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities
Curiosity and interest in understanding business challenges
Ability to work across teams, functions, and stakeholder groups

Title: Data & Marketing Analyst
Description:
At A-Players, we are building a new AI product in the mental health and mindfulness space - and we are looking for a Data & Marketing Analyst who will own the analytics layer from day one.

We are not building analytics after the fact. We are building it before the first ad goes live. That means clean tracking from day one, attribution that reflects reality, and dashboards the performance team opens every morning - not once a quarter.

In practice, you will set up the full tracking infrastructure from scratch, own the attribution model, connect marketing spend to real revenue, and keep LTV and retention metrics current and trustworthy. You will work side by side with the performance marketing team.

You will be the dedicated analyst on this product. Around you is a mature data team of 10+ people across Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Data Science, BI, and AI, as well as shared infrastructure including BigQuery, dbt, Tableau, and GA4.

This role is a fit if you have navigated the full analytics stack at a fast-moving web subscription product, know what it takes to build trust in data from scratch, and want to do meaningful work on something that is just getting started.

Responsibilities


Build the web tracking foundation for the product. Set up GA4 and GTM, both client-side and server-side, from scratch: UTM conventions, dataLayer schema, event deduplication, and attribution windows.
Implement server-side conversions for paid channels, including Meta CAPI, Google Enhanced Conversions, and TikTok Events API.
Build and maintain a web attribution model that connects marketing spend to actual revenue, and deliver channel-level LTV and CAC analysis.
Design and own Tableau dashboards for acquisition metrics, including CAC, ROAS, and channel mix; funnel metrics, including registration → first purchase → repeat purchase; retention metrics, including cohorts and LTV curves; and financial overview.
Support the performance marketing team with ad-hoc SQL analysis and Python scripts for automation.
Design and analyze A/B tests across the funnel, document results, and help drive action based on findings.
Document tracking conventions and data definitions in the knowledge base.


What We Look For


3+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, Web Analyst, or Marketing Analyst working with web subscription or e-commerce products.
Strong web analytics expertise, including funnels, attribution, tracking logic, and marketing performance measurement.
Experience working with performance marketing data.
Experience working with raw and semi-processed data from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or similar platforms, including data validation and cleanup.
Strong understanding of subscription business metrics, including LTV, cohorts, retention, recurring revenue, CAC, and payback period.
Experience setting up web tracking from scratch at least once, for example for a new product, new funnel, or new market.
Advanced SQL: window functions, CTEs, query optimization, and working with complex datasets.
Proficient Python: pandas, NumPy, analysis scripts, and basic automation.
Experience with BI tools such as Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or similar.
Comfortable working with ambiguity and building structure where it does not yet exist.
Open, clear, and proactive communication style.
High level of ownership and initiative.


Nice to have


Hands-on experience with GA4.
Hands-on experience with GTM, including client-side and server-side implementation.
Experience working with PSP data.
A/B testing experience.
Tableau experience: dashboards, calculated fields, parameter actions.
BigQuery experience: large datasets, cost optimization, and performance optimization.
Experience with dbt.


We Offer


Full ownership and freedom to set up tracking from scratch for a new product, with no legacy constraints.
Real business impact: you will work directly with a performance team running live campaigns, not build dashboards no one reads.
A strong data team around you (10+people), including Data Engineers, Data Scientists, BI specialists, and AI specialists to consult with, learn from, and grow with.
Modern stack: SQL, BigQuery, dbt, GA4, GTM, Tableau, and Python.
Openness to new practices: the team has already implemented GrowthBook, dbt, server-side tracking, and GenAI skills for analysts.
Career path: strong performance opens the door to real career growth.
Flexible schedule and remote-friendly setup.
Competitive compensation with regular performance reviews.
Medical insurance.
Learning and development budget.

Title: Business Analyst Reporting & Data Visualization
Description:
Beschreibung:

Für unseren Kunden im Banken-/Finanzumfeld suchen wir einen erfahrenen Business Analysten mit starkem Fokus auf visuell ansprechende Reports, Datenanalyse und SQL. Gesucht wird keine rein technische Entwicklerrolle, sondern eine businessnahe Persönlichkeit, welche Anforderungen aus den Fachbereichen versteht, Daten sinnvoll strukturiert und daraus verständliche, professionelle und visuell überzeugende Reports und Dashboards erstellt.




Analyse und Aufnahme von Business-Anforderungen im Reporting- und Datenumfeld
Übersetzung fachlicher Fragestellungen in aussagekräftige Reports, Dashboards und Visualisierungen
Erstellung, Weiterentwicklung und Optimierung von visuell ansprechenden Management- und Fachbereichsreports
Analyse von Datenquellen, Datenmodellen und bestehenden SQL-Abfragen
Erstellung und Optimierung von SQL-Queries zur Datenaufbereitung und Analyse
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen, IT, Data-Teams und weiteren Stakeholdern
Strukturierung und Aufbereitung komplexer Informationen für unterschiedliche Zielgruppen
Unterstützung bei der Definition von KPIs, Reporting-Logiken und Datenqualitätsanforderungen
Validierung von Reports, Plausibilisierung von Daten und Sicherstellung der fachlichen Korrektheit
Dokumentation von Anforderungen, Datenflüssen, Report-Logiken und Abnahmekriterien




Anforderung:

Mehrjährige Erfahrung als Business Analyst, idealerweise im Banken-, Finanz- oder Versicherungsumfeld
Sehr gute Erfahrung in der Erstellung von professionellen, visuell ansprechenden Reports und Dashboards
Starkes Verständnis für Reporting, KPI-Logiken, Datenvisualisierung und adressatengerechte Informationsaufbereitung
Gute bis sehr gute SQL-Kenntnisse für Datenabfragen, Datenanalyse und Plausibilisierung
Fähigkeit, fachliche Anforderungen strukturiert aufzunehmen und in konkrete Reporting-Lösungen zu übersetzen
Erfahrung in der Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen und technischen Teams
Ausgeprägtes Gespür für klare, verständliche und optisch saubere Darstellung von Daten
Selbstständige, strukturierte und analytische Arbeitsweise
Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse zwingend erforderlich
Englischkenntnisse von Vorteil

Title: Internship Environmental Data Analysis
Description:
Mission

Within EPFL Sustainability, part of the Vice-Presidency for Research and Strategic Initiatives, your role will be to support the department’s activities. In particular, you will assist the Environmental Data Officer with her ad hoc analyses and cross-functional projects

Main Duties And Responsibilities


Collaborate on the collection, cleaning, structuring and documentation of environmental data, ensuring its traceability and quality
Process and analyse data using Excel and Python
Contribute to the preparation of EPFL’s carbon footprint assessment and that of laboratory activities
Contribute to the development of the CO2 Calculator project in terms of calculation methodology and data
Contribute to analyses aimed at studying the environmental impact and the progress of sustainability practices at EPFL
Contribute to the drafting of communication content on sustainability results and initiatives (annual reports, dashboards, infographics)


Profile


Master’s degree in environmental engineering, computational science, mathematics, data science, or a related field (the internship must begin within 12 months of graduation).
Advanced skills in Excel (complex formulas, VBA) and Python (pandas, numpy, visualisation) for data processing and analysis
Experience with best practices in versioning and code management (Git, GitHub/GitLab, etc.)
Experience with data visualisation tools (Power BI, Tableau, etc.) would be an advantage
Knowledge of SimaPro or other life cycle assessment (LCA) software desirable
Strong interest in sustainability issues, particularly in academic research.
Creativity, organisational rigour and initiative.


A collaborative spirit and the ability to adapt

We offer


The opportunity to contribute to stimulating projects aimed at reducing environmental impacts within an academic institution
A flexible working environment within a dynamic and multidisciplinary team


Informations

Contract Start Date : Mid-july/August 2026

Activity Rate Min : 100

Activity Rate Max : 100

Contract Type: CDD

Duration: 6 months

Reference

Contact: Martina Gallato, Environmental Data Officer

Application: Please send your CV and cover letter by 26 June 2026 to martina.gallato@epfl.ch

  • Contribute to the drafting of communication content on sustainability results and initiatives (annual reports, dashboards, infographics)

Profile

  • Master’s degree in environmental engineering, computational science, mathematics, data science, or a related field (the internship must begin within 12 months of graduation).
  • Advanced skills in Excel (complex formulas, VBA) and Python (pandas, numpy, visualisation) for data processing and analysis
  • Experience with best practices in versioning and code management (Git, GitHub/GitLab, etc.)
  • Experience with data visualisation tools (Power BI, Tableau, etc.) would be an advantage
  • Knowledge of SimaPro or other life cycle assessment (LCA) software desirable
  • Strong interest in sustainability issues, particularly in academic research.
  • Creativity, organisational rigour and initiative.

A collaborative spirit and the ability to adapt

We offer

  • The opportunity to contribute to stimulating projects aimed at reducing environmental impacts within an academic institution
  • A flexible working environment within a dynamic and multidisciplinary team

Informations

Contract Start Date : Mid-july/August 2026

Activity Rate Min : 100

Activity Rate Max : 100

Contract Type: CDD

Duration: 6 months

Reference

Contact: Martina Gallato, Environmental Data Officer

Application: Please send your CV and cover letter by 26 June 2026 to martina.gallato@epfl.ch

Title: Product Analyst
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Product Analyst based in Switzerland.

This role sits at the core of product decision-making, turning user behavior and platform data into clear, actionable insights that guide product strategy and roadmap priorities.

You will analyze how customers interact with a complex SaaS platform, identifying adoption patterns, friction points, and retention signals that directly influence product evolution.

Working closely with Product leadership and cross-functional teams, you will help ensure decisions are grounded in data rather than assumptions.

The environment is fast-moving and highly analytical, with strong exposure to senior stakeholders and product strategy discussions.

You will play a key role in strengthening product intelligence capabilities across the organization, improving visibility into what drives user value.

This position is ideal for someone who enjoys combining deep analytical work with direct impact on product direction and business outcomes.

It offers a highly collaborative setting where insights are expected to translate into real product improvements.

Accountabilities


Own user behavior analysis across the product, including journeys, engagement patterns, session depth, and usage frequency across different customer segments.
Monitor feature performance, adoption rates, and experimentation results to identify what drives or limits product value.
Conduct cohort, funnel, retention, and expansion analyses to uncover behavioral trends and predict customer lifecycle outcomes.
Identify friction points, drop-offs, and underperforming areas, translating findings into structured, actionable recommendations.
Define, track, and maintain core product KPIs, ensuring consistency, accuracy, and reliability across reporting layers.
Provide ongoing analytical input to Product leadership, supporting roadmap decisions and early detection of product health risks.


Requirements


3-6+ years of experience in product analytics, SaaS analytics, or data-driven product environments.
Strong proficiency in SQL, data modeling, and analytics tools, with hands-on experience working with large datasets.
Solid experience in cohort analysis, funnel analysis, retention modeling, and behavioral data interpretation.
Proven ability to translate complex data into clear, executive-ready insights that influence product decisions.
Experience working closely with Product Managers and cross-functional teams in high-growth environments.
Strong quantitative reasoning, structured thinking, and ability to operate with incomplete or evolving data.
Comfortable in fast-paced, product-led organizations where insights directly shape execution.


Benefits


Competitive compensation aligned with experience and market benchmarks
Fully remote work environment with global collaboration opportunities
Flexible working arrangements supporting work-life balance
Opportunity to work closely with senior product leadership and influence roadmap decisions
Exposure to large-scale SaaS product analytics and strategic decision-making
Dynamic, fast-growing environment focused on data-driven innovation


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Internship Global Master Data Analyst 80-100%
Description:
Switzerland, Visp

The Master Data department in Visp is a global team responsible for the development of master data processes and standards that are to be followed by the whole organization.

As a part of the Global Master Data team, you will be able to learn SAP Materials Management (MM), Production & Planning (PP) and Sales & Distribution (SD) modules, key business processes as well as SAP data structures. Overall, you will interact with many different departments and experts across the company worldwide.

What You Will Get


A dynamic, agile career path with room to grow
An inclusive, ethical, and supportive workplace culture
A comprehensive benefits package tailored to your role and location


What You Will Do

As an intern, you will play an active role in:


Perform various data analyses and reports to support Master data or Business projects
Assisting with SAP Master Data mass maintenance and migration efforts
Gathering feedback from global and local Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) across the organization
Defining functional and technical requirements for the development or enhancement of Master Data processes
Updating and improving training materials, and delivering hands-on training sessions to teams across Lonza


What We Are Looking For


A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree (or equivalent experience) in data science, computer science, business information technology, or mathematics
Solid understanding of data structures or hands-on experience with databases
Proficiency in English; German and other languages are a valuable plus
A passion for optimizing processes, improving IT tools, and exploring innovative solutions
You are interested in being a reliable team member in a multi-cultural environment.


About Lonza

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Title: Data Specialist
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Data Specialist based in Switzerland.

This role is focused on owning and evolving end-to-end data infrastructure, analytics, and reporting in a fully remote, global environment. You will be responsible for building reliable data pipelines, designing scalable dashboards, and ensuring the accuracy and integrity of all business-critical reporting. Acting as a key enabler of data-driven decision-making, you will work closely with engineering and business teams to transform raw data into clear, actionable insights. The environment is fast-moving and highly autonomous, requiring strong ownership, precision, and proactive problem-solving. You will also play a central role in identifying anomalies, improving data quality, and strengthening analytics systems across the organization. This is a high-impact position where your work directly influences strategic decisions and operational performance.

Accountabilities


Manage and optimize end-to-end data pipelines and data warehouse systems using ETL tools such as Stitch and Google BigQuery.
Design, build, and maintain dashboards and reporting systems using Looker Studio to support business intelligence needs across teams.
Ensure accurate implementation and monitoring of Google Analytics (GA4), including event tracking, validation, and reporting consistency.
Execute rigorous data validation and QA processes to guarantee accuracy, consistency, and reliability of all reporting outputs.
Proactively monitor data flows to detect anomalies, identify issues, and implement corrective actions before they impact decision-making.
Develop custom, on-demand reports and analyses for cross-functional business teams.
Collaborate with engineering and stakeholders to improve data architecture, tracking systems, and reporting frameworks.
Maintain strong documentation, task tracking, and structured workflows using tools such as Trello and Slack.


Requirements


3+ years of experience in a Data Analyst, Data Specialist, or analytics-focused role.
Hands-on experience with data pipelines and ETL tools such as Stitch or similar, and data warehousing in Google BigQuery.
Strong proficiency in Looker Studio, including dashboard creation and advanced calculated metrics or reporting logic.
Experience with Google Analytics (GA4 preferred), including event tracking, implementation, and interpretation of behavioral data.
Proven ability to validate datasets, QA dashboards, and ensure end-to-end data accuracy.
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to detect trends, anomalies, and actionable insights from complex datasets.
Experience delivering custom reports and insights for cross-functional teams in fast-paced environments.
Excellent organizational skills with the ability to document work and manage tasks independently in a remote setup.
Strong communication skills, with responsiveness and clarity in distributed team environments.


Benefits


Fully remote-first work environment with global flexibility
Opportunity to directly influence data-driven decision-making across the organization
High autonomy with ownership over end-to-end analytics and data systems
Access to modern data stack technologies and tools
Agile, low-bureaucracy environment focused on execution and impact
Strong emphasis on experimentation and continuous improvement
Collaborative global team culture across multiple time zones
Opportunity to build scalable analytics systems from the ground up

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Who we are




SII Group is a trusted technology partner, SII provides high value added solutions for the IT projects of many large corporations. Since its founding in 1979, the Company has been providing solutions adapted to its clients’ needs, by relying on :




> Its acknowledged expertise in various industries and sectors

> Proven 'turnkey' solutions

> An efficient quality system

> Adaptable, evolving services




Today, with a staff of more than 18'000, SII Group is supporting companies across 20 countries.




At SII Switzerland we pay attention to the personal and professional wellbeing of our employees. We place our collaborators at the heart of our actions and activities. If you are motivated by the perspective of joining a big, trusted and recognized group, then let's meet !




To develop our consulting offer and support one of our clients, we are looking for a talented Data Engineer.




Missions

Develop & Scale Pipelines: You design and implement robust ETL/ELT pipelines to process large, complex volumes of data (Big Data) using both code-based and visual tools.
Semantic Data Modeling: You translate complex business processes into logical data models and help build a centralized, cross-departmental enterprise data model.
Data Quality & Lineage: You implement automated quality checks and monitor data flows from source to end product to guarantee seamless data lineage.
Code & Transformation: You write clean, high-performance code in PySpark and SQL to clean, aggregate, and prepare data for downstream analysis.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: You work closely with Data Scientists, Business Analysts, and business departments to quickly translate their requirements into production-ready data structures.




Profile

Initial practical experience in Data Engineering (as a Junior, e.g., through relevant internships/working student roles; as a Mid-Level, approx. 1–3 years of professional experience).
Solid knowledge of Python (especially in a Big Data context with PySpark) as well as proficiency in writing complex SQL queries.
Good understanding of modern data architectures (Data Lakehouse, distributed systems) and ideally experience with integrated all-in-one data platforms.
Familiar with relational and non-relational databases. You enjoy bringing order to unstructured mountains of data.
Analytical mindset, work in a structured manner, and possess a strong willingness to learn, allowing you to quickly adapt to new, complex software ecosystems.




For information

Start date : September
Location : Zurich - CH
Duration : permanent contract
Language : fluent in English, German is a plus

Description:
Who we are




SII Group is a trusted technology partner, SII provides high value added solutions for the IT projects of many large corporations. Since its founding in 1979, the Company has been providing solutions adapted to its clients’ needs, by relying on :




> Its acknowledged expertise in various industries and sectors

At SII Switzerland we pay attention to the personal and professional wellbeing of our employees. We place our collaborators at the heart of our actions and activities. If you are motivated by the perspective of joining a big, trusted and recognized group, then let's meet !




To develop our consulting offer and support one of our clients, we are looking for a talented Data Engineer (Palantir).




Missions

Build Pipelines in Foundry: You develop and scale data pipelines (ETL/ELT) directly within Palantir Foundry (e.g., via Code Repositories, Pipeline Builder) and integrate data from a wide variety of source systems.
Data Modeling & Ontology: You help map our data into the Foundry Ontology, making complex business logic tangible and accessible for analysts and end-users.
Data Quality & Lineage: You implement health checks, monitor data quality across the entire data lineage process, and ensure that pipelines run stably.
Code & Transformation: You write clean, efficient code (primarily in PySpark and SQL) to clean and transform large volumes of data.
Team Collaboration: You work closely with Data Scientists, Analysts, and business departments to translate their requirements into functioning data models within Foundry.




Profile

Initial professional experience in Data Engineering (as a Junior, e.g., through relevant internships/working student roles; as a Mid-Level, approx. 1–3 years in a permanent role).

Solution-oriented, think analytically, and enjoy diving into complex datasets and learning new tools.

Title: Data Engineer - HR Data & Analytics
Description:
Sika is a specialty chemicals company with a globally leading position in the development and production of systems and products for bonding, sealing, damping, reinforcing, and protection in the building sector and automotive industry. Sika has subsidiaries in 103 countries around the world and, in over 400 factories, produces innovative technologies for customers worldwide. In doing so, it plays a crucial role in enabling the transformation of the construction and vehicle industries toward greater environmental compatibility. With more than 33,000 employees, the company generated sales of CHF 11.2 billion in 2023.





Job Description



Sika is expanding rapidly, and our data requirements are growing just as fast. To meet these needs, we are shaping a global strategy for how Sika manages and leverages analytical data, and we are building a state-of-the-art cloud-based data analytics platform.



Join our Central Data & Analytics Team as a Data Engineer - HR Data & Analytics in Zürich, where you will play a pivotal role in designing and implementing innovative solutions for data engineering and analytics. In this position, you will directly influence the architecture and execution of our new global data analytics approach, helping to set the standard for how Sika utilizes data to drive business value. Collaborate closely with HR, People Analytics, and HRIS teams to translate HR metrics and use cases into data models and reusable data products.



Your Impact During the First 6 Months



Build the Foundations



Develop a deep understanding of Sika's data architecture, HR technology landscape, and Data Mesh strategy.
Build strong partnerships with HR, HRIS, People Analytics, and Data & Analytics teams.
Assess existing HR data assets and identify opportunities for improvement, scalability, and automation.

Design & Deliver



Design, develop, and maintain scalable ETL/ELT pipelines integrating data from systems such as SuccessFactors, SmartRecruiters, Rise Up, and other enterprise applications.
Build reusable data models and data products that support reporting, analytics, and AI use cases.
Implement data quality, monitoring, security, and governance standards to ensure trusted and compliant HR data.

Scale & Optimize



Deliver high-quality data products for workforce analytics, talent acquisition, learning, engagement, and future AI initiatives.
Optimize pipelines and data models for performance, reliability, scalability, and cost efficiency.
Contribute to the evolution of Sika's global data platform, engineering standards, and analytics capabilities


Qualifications



Education



Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Relevant Data Engineering, Cloud, or Analytics certifications are highly valued.

Experience



Several years of professional experience in Data Engineering, Analytics Engineering, or a related field.
Experience supporting HR transformation, People Analytics, HRIS, or workforce-related data initiatives is highly desirable.
Strong understanding of modern data architectures, including Data Mesh, Data Products, and Domain-Oriented Data Ownership.
Experience designing analytical data models, dimensional models, and star schemas for enterprise reporting environments.
Demonstrated experience delivering production-grade data pipelines and data platforms in cloud environments.

Technical Expertise



Strong experience with Microsoft Azure technologies, including:
Azure Databricks
Azure Data Factory
Azure Storage
Azure Key Vault
Azure SQL and related services
Advanced proficiency in:
SQL
Python
PySpark
dbt
Experience with source control and collaborative development using Azure DevOps and/or GitHub.
Strong understanding of CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure as Code, testing frameworks, and DevOps practices.
Experience supporting Power BI datasets and enterprise reporting environments.
Knowledge of data governance, data quality management, metadata management, and security frameworks.

Personal Competencies



Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to translate business requirements into scalable technical solutions.
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.
Comfortable working in cross-functional and international environments.
Self-driven, structured, and able to manage multiple priorities.
Curious about emerging technologies, AI, and the future of data-driven organizations.

Additional Information



Location: Zurich, Switzerland
Hybrid work model: 4 days on-site, 1 day remote
No visa sponsorship available for this position







Additional Information



Building Trust every day



We offer intensive training and interesting perspectives for anyone who wants to build a career
The chance to bring new ideas, interesting areas for development, and taking responsibility early on are some of the key factors that drive Sika's pioneering spirit
Friendly, personable and often surprisingly uncomplicated, that's how many of our employees describe their working relationships at Sika. We call this the Sika Spirit

For insights into what our colleagues say about working at Sika, please click here.



We look forward to your direct online application.



We offer competitive salaries, aligned with local market benchmarks and the specific scope and responsibilities of each role. Compensation is determined based skills relevant to the position, education and/or training. We are committed to fair and equitable pay practices in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.

Title: Sr. Data Analyst, Growth
Description:
Building the Future of Open Finance

Payward - the parent company behind Kraken, NinjaTrader, Breakout, xStocks, Payward Services and CF Benchmarks - has spent the last 15 years building one of the most modern and globally accessible financial infrastructure platforms in the industry, built to advance an open, global financial system.

Before you apply, we encourage you to explore our culture page to understand what drives us and how we work.

The team

Founded in 2011, Kraken is one of the world's longest-standing crypto platforms, trusted by over 10 million individuals and institutions across the globe. It offers spot trading, margin, futures, staking, and OTC services, with products built for both individual investors and institutional clients.

At Kraken, the data team is the infrastructure layer beneath every product decision. We're not looking for someone to report on what happened. We're looking for someone who builds the systems that make it possible to know at all and then uses them to shape what happens next.

We're looking for a Senior Data Analyst, Growth to join our team remotely. This is a staff-level individual contributor role where you will own the Growth domain end to end. You won't just be answering questions. You'll be deciding which questions are worth asking, building the infrastructure to answer them at scale, and influencing the technical direction of the team along the way. Experimentation is core to how we work. We run rigorous A/B tests, apply causal inference at scale and treat experimentation as a first-class discipline, not an afterthought.

You'll own production-grade pipelines, dbt models, dashboards and metric frameworks. You'll define north star metrics, drive experimentation infrastructure and translate results into clear business recommendations. You'll also play an active role in shaping how AI tooling is embedded into how we work, from LLM-augmented pipelines to GenAI-assisted workflows that have real, measurable impact on the business.

At this level, we expect more and we expect it faster. You'll operate with a high degree of autonomy, move quickly without sacrificing quality, and set a bar that raises the team around you.

The Opportunity


Operate as a full-stack data analyst within the Growth team, owning your domain completely while collaborating closely with colleagues across the pod.
Own the design and evolution of dashboards, north star metrics and analytical frameworks that drive decisions at the highest level of the business.
Build and maintain data infrastructure at scale, from scalable dbt models and production pipelines to full-funnel reporting that powers cross-functional teams.
Lead experimentation across the Growth domain by designing and owning A/B testing frameworks, applying causal inference techniques and turning results into clear, confident recommendations that influence product and growth strategy.
Influence technical direction across the data team, contributing to how we build, what we prioritise and how we raise the bar on data quality and engineering standards.
Embed AI tooling into your workflow in ways that have tangible business impact, including LLM-augmented pipelines and GenAI-assisted analytics workflows.
Deliver insights through clear, data-driven storytelling to technical and non-technical audiences, including senior leadership.


What You Bring


10+ years of experience in data analytics or analytics engineering, ideally within fintech, payments, crypto or a high-scale marketplace where data quality and scale are non-negotiable.
Proven track record of building and owning data infrastructure at scale, not just using it. You've built the pipelines, defined the models and taken ownership of what happens when things break.
Deep expertise with dbt is a must. You've used it in production, you know its limits and you've built models that others depend on.
Strong experience managing ELT/ETL pipelines end to end, with hands-on familiarity with Airflow or equivalent orchestration tools.
Full mastery of SQL and strong Python proficiency for pipeline development, analysis and production-grade code.
Deep hands-on experience designing, running and owning experimentation programmes including A/B testing frameworks and causal inference at scale. You've built the infrastructure, not just used it, and you can translate results into clear business decisions.
Experience deploying AI tools including LLM-augmented pipelines or GenAI-assisted workflows with demonstrable business impact.
A track record of influencing technical direction and data strategy, not just executing within it.
Strong communicator who can simplify complex data ideas for both technical and non-technical audiences, including senior leadership.
A degree in a field emphasising analytical rigour such as software engineering, economics or a hard science.
Based in Canada, the US, the UK or the EU and fluent in English.


Nice to haves


Solid understanding of the cryptocurrency space and how financial markets function


Unless a specific application deadline is stated in the job posting, applications are accepted on an ongoing basis.

Please note, applicants are permitted to redact or remove information on their resume that identifies age, date of birth, or dates of attendance at or graduation from an educational institution.

We consider qualified applicants with criminal histories for employment on our team, assessing candidates in a manner consistent with the requirements of the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance.

Our commitment

Payward is powered by people from around the world and we celebrate the diverse talents, backgrounds, contributions, and unique perspectives that everyone brings to the table. We hire based on merit, seeking out people with the right abilities, knowledge, and skills for the job. We encourage you to apply for roles where you don't fully meet the listed requirements, especially if you're passionate or knowledgeable about crypto.

We may ask candidates to complete job-related skills or work-style assessments as part of our hiring process. These assessments evaluate competencies relevant to the role and are applied consistently across candidates for similar positions. Results are considered alongside experience and interviews, and are not the sole basis for any employment decision.

As an equal opportunity employer, we don't tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind, whether based on race, ethnicity, age, gender identity, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state, or local laws.
