Title: Data Engineer
Description:
We are looking for a Senior Data Analytics Engineer who can take ownership of tasks, deliver stable, incremental changes frequently, and ensure thorough testing to maintain high-quality standards.




This role tackles a range of complex software and data challenges, including data management, advanced analytics, and business intelligence. It is pivotal in implementing, maintaining, and supporting data pipelines using shared data platforms that exploit modern data technologies and software development practices




Tasks & Responsibilities:

• Participate and contribute to overall data architecture and design.

• Ensure effective design and development of system architectures through frequent product deliveries, employing effective governance methods for transparency and communication.

• Translate functional and technical requirements into detailed design.

• Develop required code for end-to-end data pipelines with optimal extraction, transformation, and loading of data from a wide variety of data sources. Incorporate business rules and functional capabilities, security, data retention requirements, etc.

• Work with stakeholders including business area sponsors, product owners, data architects, data engineers, project managers, and business analysts to assist them with their data-related technical issues and support their data needs.

• Remain up to date with industry standards and technological advancements that will improve the quality of your output.

• Interact with the business to identify, capture, and analyse business requirements.

• Assist and support proof of concepts as data technology evolves.




Must-Haves:

• Minimum of 7 years of work experience in modern data technologies as a Data Analytics Engineer or similar role.

• Experience in building data ingestion pipelines for data lake and data warehouse architecture. As well as strong experience in data modelling, design patterns, and building highly scalable applications.

• Hands-on development using open-source data technologies such as Hadoop, Hive, Spark, HBase, Kafka, Impala, ELK, etc., preferably with Cloudera.

• Experience working with relational databases such as Oracle, SQL Server, etc.

• Strong experience with Python

HTML:
We are looking for a Senior Data Analytics Engineer who can take ownership of tasks, deliver stable, incremental changes frequently, and ensure thorough testing to maintain high-quality standards.


This role tackles a range of complex software and data challenges, including data management, advanced analytics, and business intelligence. It is pivotal in implementing, maintaining, and supporting data pipelines using shared data platforms that exploit modern data technologies and software development practices


Tasks & Responsibilities:

• Participate and contribute to overall data architecture and design.

• Ensure effective design and development of system architectures through frequent product deliveries, employing effective governance methods for transparency and communication.

• Translate functional and technical requirements into detailed design.

• Develop required code for end-to-end data pipelines with optimal extraction, transformation, and loading of data from a wide variety of data sources. Incorporate business rules and functional capabilities, security, data retention requirements, etc.

• Work with stakeholders including business area sponsors, product owners, data architects, data engineers, project managers, and business analysts to assist them with their data-related technical issues and support their data needs.

• Remain up to date with industry standards and technological advancements that will improve the quality of your output.

• Interact with the business to identify, capture, and analyse business requirements.

• Assist and support proof of concepts as data technology evolves.


Must-Haves:

• Minimum of 7 years of work experience in modern data technologies as a Data Analytics Engineer or similar role.

• Experience in building data ingestion pipelines for data lake and data warehouse architecture. As well as strong experience in data modelling, design patterns, and building highly scalable applications.

• Hands-on development using open-source data technologies such as Hadoop, Hive, Spark, HBase, Kafka, Impala, ELK, etc., preferably with Cloudera.

• Experience working with relational databases such as Oracle, SQL Server, etc.

• Strong experience with Python

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Data Engineer (Modern Data Platforms)

Start Date: July/August 2026
Duration: 24 months
Workload: 100%


Main Responsibilities:
Design, develop, and maintain end-to-end data pipelines (ETL/ELT) using Spark, SQL, and related technologies.
Implement business logic, data governance, security, and retention requirements within pipelines.
Translate business and technical requirements into scalable data solutions and detailed designs.
Collaborate with business stakeholders, product owners, and technical teams to address data needs and issues.
Contribute to data architecture, ensuring scalable and high-performance system design.
Support continuous delivery through effective governance, transparency, and communication.
Stay up to date with emerging data technologies and support proof-of-concept initiatives.
Required Background:
5+ years of experience working with modern data technologies.
Strong experience building data ingestion pipelines for data warehouse, data lake, or lakehouse architectures.
Hands-on expertise with big data and open-source tools (e.g., Spark, Hadoop, Hive, Kafka, dbt, ELK), ideally in a Cloudera environment.
Proficiency in Python and strong SQL skills.
Solid understanding of data modelling, scalable system design, and data engineering best practices.
Experience in financial services (e.g., trade lifecycle, risk management).
Familiarity with lakehouse technologies such as Apache Iceberg.
Experience with relational and NoSQL databases (e.g., Postgres, SQL Server, Cassandra).
Exposure to workflow orchestration (Airflow, NiFi), streaming (Kafka, Spark Streaming), and CI/CD pipelines.
Knowledge of Agile methodologies and testing practices.
Experience with cloud platforms (Databricks, Snowflake, Azure Synapse) - nice-to-have
Understanding of microservices and APIs (REST/SOAP)  - nice-to-have
Exposure to .NET/C#  - nice-to-have
You bring strong communication skills 



We believe in equal opportunity for all and actively encourage applications from diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives.
Source Group International Ltd is acting as an Employment Business in relation to this vacancy

Title: Working Student — Fleet Observability & Data Visualization
Description:
Gravis Robotics is a startup turning heavy construction machines into intelligent and autonomous robots. Our unique combination of learning-based automation and augmented remote control enables a single operator to safely manage a fleet of earthmoving machines in a gamified environment. With over a decade of academic experience at the cutting edge of large-scale robotics, our team is rapidly translating this expertise into real-world deployments with industry leaders in a trillion-dollar market.

At Gravis, we operate at the intersection of hardware, software, and real-world deployment. Our Rooftop Autonomous Control Kit (RACK) integrates sensing, compute, communication, and networking into a manufacturer-agnostic solution deployable across a wide range of construction machines.

We are looking for a motivated working student to join our team for an extended engagement (several months or longer), contributing 1 to 2 days per week. Our office is located in Zurich city, close to Bahnhof Oerlikon, and is easily reachable by public transport. You will own the pipeline that turns raw telemetry from our deployed machines into actionable insight — building dashboards that the whole company can read at a glance, and automating the analysis that catches problems before they reach the field engineers. This is a great opportunity to shape how a fast-growing robotics company sees its own fleet.

Your Responsibilities


Build and maintain pipelines to fetch metrics from machines deployed in the field
Organize and structure operational data so it can be visualized
Design and implement live dashboards on our office display to distribute the information company wide
Set up automated analysis of trends and long-term averages across different metrics
Develop anomaly-detection workflows to flag decalibration, sensor drift, or unexpected behavior before it becomes an incident
Leverage AI agents to accelerate data exploration, summarization, and routine analysis tasks



What You Will Gain


Hands-on experience with the full data lifecycle of a production robotics fleet — from raw telemetry to actionable insight
Direct visibility into how a deep-tech startup uses data to scale up its operations
The opportunity to take ownership of a system that the whole company relies on every day



Required Qualifications


EU/EFTA students
Currently enrolled at a Swiss university (Bachelor's or Master's level), ideally in computer science, data science, electrical engineering, robotics, or a related field
Proficiency in Linux systems and comfort working from the command line
Experience with observability and visualization tools such as Grafana, Prometheus, Foxglove, Kibana, Datadog, or comparable stacks
Working knowledge of modern web technologies (HTML/CSS, JavaScript/TypeScript, and a frontend framework such as React, Vue, or Svelte)
Working knowledge of git
Reliable, detail-oriented, and comfortable owning a project end-to-end
Good communication skills in English



Nice to Have


Experience with time-series databases (Prometheus, InfluxDB, TimescaleDB, VictoriaMetrics, or similar)
Familiarity with statistical methods for anomaly detection or trend analysis Experience with data pipelines, message brokers, or stream processing (MQTT, Kafka, Zenoh, ROS bags, or similar)
Familiarity with robotics concepts (sensors, coordinate frames, calibration, etc.)
Practical experience using AI agents or LLM-based tools in a development or analysis workflow
Prior experience building dashboards or monitoring systems (ideally in production)
Long-term engagement preferred, with the possibility to grow your responsibilities over time



This is an opportunity to join a dynamic and versatile team, and to be part of a young startup that will revolutionize heavy construction. You will get the chance to apply your skills to critical tasks, while learning from a team of world-class robotic engineers. Gravis Robotics offers a fair market salary and an excellent working location in the city of Zurich.

Gravis is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to inclusion and diversity, and do not discriminate based upon race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, gender identity, gender expression, disability, veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics. All qualified people are highly encouraged to apply.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Expert Power BI Senior
Description:
📣 Talent Access Technologies SA – Qui sommes-nous ?

TAT développe en Suisse Romande un modèle de conseil différent :

nous réunissons des consultants seniors reconnus pour leur expertise, qui souhaitent intervenir sur des projets à forte valeur ajoutée tout en participant au développement d’un cabinet en croissance.

Dans le cadre du développement et de l'évolution d'une plateforme décisionnelle stratégique, nous recherchons un(e) Expert Power BI Senior pour accompagner les métiers dans la valorisation de leurs données et la création d'indicateurs de pilotage à forte valeur ajoutée.




📌 Responsabilités du poste




Concevoir, développer et faire évoluer des tableaux de bord Power BI.
Modéliser et transformer les données (Dataflows, modèles sémantiques, Power Query).
Assurer l'administration et le bon fonctionnement de la plateforme Power BI.
Participer à l'analyse des besoins métiers et à la définition des indicateurs de performance.
Accompagner les utilisateurs via du support, du conseil et de la formation.
Contribuer à l'amélioration continue, à l'optimisation des performances et à la veille technologique.
Collaborer avec les équipes projets pour prioriser et mettre en œuvre les évolutions fonctionnelles.




💼 Votre profil

Formation

Excellente maîtrise de Power BI.
Expérience confirmée sur les Dataflows et les modèles sémantiques.
Solides compétences en DAX, Power Query et langage M.
Expérience de Microsoft Fabric appréciée.
Bonne compréhension des problématiques de gouvernance et de qualité des données.
Expérience dans la conception de tableaux de bord ergonomiques et orientés utilisateurs.
Bachelor en informatique ou expérience équivalente.
Certification Microsoft Fabric ou certifications BI appréciées.




🏁 L’esprit intrapreneur que nous recherchons

Chez TAT, nous valorisons les consultants qui souhaitent avoir un impact au-delà de leur mission.

Nous recherchons des profils qui :

Développent une relation de confiance avec leurs clients
Identifient naturellement des opportunités d’amélioration ou de transformation
Ont envie de contribuer au développement d’une société de conseil à taille humaine
Combinent excellence opérationnelle et esprit entrepreneurial




Les consultants qui le souhaitent peuvent ainsi participer activement au développement du cabinet et bénéficier d’un modèle intrapreneurial structuré récompensant cette contribution, en complément de leur rémunération de base.




🏆 Pourquoi nous rejoindre ?

Missions à forte valeur ajoutée auprès de clients de premier plan
Environnement très agile et entrepreneurial
Forte autonomie et reconnaissance des consultants
Modèle permettant aux consultants de bénéficier directement du développement du business

Postulez dès maintenant sur TalentAccess.ch ou envoyez votre candidature à Candidatures@talentaccess.ch

Nous avons hâte de découvrir votre talent !

Title: Digital Data Analyst Consultant, Individual Giving, PFP, Geneva, Switzerland, 12 months (Remote) #593914
Description:
UNICEF works in over 190 countries and territories to save children’s lives, defend their rights, and help them fulfill their potential, from early childhood through adolescence.

At UNICEF, we are committed, passionate, and proud of what we do for as long as we are needed. Promoting the rights of every child is not just a job – it is a calling.

UNICEF is a place where careers are built. We offer our staff diverse opportunities for professional and personal development that will help them reinforce a sense of purpose while serving children and communities across the world. We welcome everyone who wants to belong and grow in a diverse and passionate culture, coupled with an attractive compensation and benefits package.

Visit our website to learn more about what we do at UNICEF.

How can you make a difference? 

UNICEF is one of the most admired organizations globally, impacting the lives of children in almost every country of the world. Your work with UNICEF will enable this life-saving and critical work in the most direct way: by raising money that lets us help the most difficult to reach.

UNICEF is at an exciting time as it expands its digital footprint, and we need great professionals to support this challenge. This role is an opportunity to take UNICEF’s fundraising to the next level. Come and help save and nurture children’s lives.

UNICEF is looking for a Digital Data Analyst to work closely with our Global Data and Insights team, which sits within the Individual Giving Unit (IG), Division of Private Fundraising and Partnerships (PFP), to support a digital transformation journey aimed at increasing revenues and making an impact on children’s lives around the world. The Digital Data Analyst will support the IG team remotely, a highly committed and performance-driven team, responsible for overseeing a fundraising program that delivers over one billion dollars of income every year.

The Digital Data Analyst will be responsible for implementing and developing complete and complex data models for donor acquisition and retention that will become a global standard for UNICEF IG team. With those data models as the foundation the Digital Data Analyst will be creating other data products like Dashboards, Machine Learning (ML) models and will ensure that all products are future-proof and AI-ready.

The Digital Data Analyst Is Expected To


Act as a subject matter expert in data analytics and GCP/Salesforce data technologies for digital channels.
Work with client teams to design and implement modern, scalable data solutions using a range of new and emerging technologies from the Google Cloud Platform and Databricks.
Work within an Agile environment.
Build and deliver Data and reporting solutions using GCP products and offerings.
Build and deliver data models that enable integrated donor feature base which will enable powerful propensity models based on multiple channel and behavioral data point that are easily replicable.


If you would like to know more about this consultancy, please review the complete Terms of Reference here: ToR Digital Data Analyst Consultant.pdf

To qualify as an advocate for every child you will have:

Minimum Requirements

Education: Bachelor’s degree in in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, IT, or any other related field.

Work Experience


5 years of progressively responsible professional work experience in data and analytics


Knowledge/Expertise/Skills


Experience in cloud technologies, in particular Google BigQuery is required.
Strong SQL knowledge and experience working with relational and non-relational databases is required.
Strong communication skills are required, with the ability to convey complex information in a clear and understandable manner.
Experience in building analytics dashboards and using data to drive insights for campaign optimization is highly desirable.


Language Requirements


Fluency in English is required. Knowledge of other official UN language (Arabic, Chinese, French, Russian or Spanish) is considered as an asset.


Desirables


A strong willingness to learn new technologies and stay up to date with trends in the fundraising space
Ability to explain marketing solutions and data to non-technical colleagues


For every Child, you demonstrate...

UNICEF’s Core Values of Care, Respect, Integrity, Trust and Accountability and Sustainability (CRITAS) underpin everything we do and how we do it. Get acquainted with Our Values Charter: UNICEF Values

UNICEF promotes and advocates for the protection of the rights of every child, everywhere, in everything it does and is mandated to support the realization of the rights of every child, including those most disadvantaged, and our global workforce must reflect the diversity of those children. The UNICEF family is committed to include everyone, irrespective of their race/ethnicity, disability, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, nationality, socio-economic background, minority, or any other status.

UNICEF encourages applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of gender, nationality, religious or ethnic backgrounds, and from people with disabilities, including neurodivergence. We offer reasonable accommodation for persons with disabilities. throughout the recruitment process. If you require any accommodation, please submit your request through the accessibility email button on the UNICEF Careers webpage Accessibility | UNICEF. Should you be shortlisted, please get in touch with the recruiter directly to share further details, enabling us to make the necessary arrangements in advance.

UNICEF does not hire candidates who are married to children (persons under 18). UNICEF has a zero-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of the United Nations and UNICEF, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and discrimination based on gender, nationality, age, race, sexual orientation, religious or ethnic background or disabilities. UNICEF is committed to promote the protection and safeguarding of all children. All selected candidates will, therefore, undergo rigorous reference and background checks, and will be expected to adhere to these standards and principles. Background checks will include the verification of academic credential(s) and employment history. Selected candidates may be required to provide additional information to conduct a background check, and selected candidates with disabilities may be requested to submit supporting documentation in relation to their disability confidentially.

Qualified candidates are invited to submit the following documents via the online recruitment portal, TMS (Talent Management System):


An up-to-date TMS profile and curriculum vitae (CV)
Cover letter
A separate financial proposal (only acceptable in the format of the linked template): Financial proposal xxx3338.docx


Remarks: If the TOR or financial proposal documents are not visible on certain recruitment platforms, please visit our official page Vacancies | UNICEF Careers.

UNICEF does not charge a processing fee at any stage of its recruitment, selection, and hiring processes (i.e., application stage, interview stage, validation stage, or appointment and training). UNICEF will not ask for applicants’ bank account information.

All UNICEF positions are advertised, and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted and advance to the next stage of the selection process.

HTML:
UNICEF works in over 190 countries and territories to save children’s lives, defend their rights, and help them fulfill their potential, from early childhood through adolescence.

At UNICEF, we are committed, passionate, and proud of what we do for as long as we are needed. Promoting the rights of every child is not just a job – it is a calling.

UNICEF is a place where careers are built. We offer our staff diverse opportunities for professional and personal development that will help them reinforce a sense of purpose while serving children and communities across the world. We welcome everyone who wants to belong and grow in a diverse and passionate culture, coupled with an attractive compensation and benefits package.

Visit our website to learn more about what we do at UNICEF.

How can you make a difference? 

UNICEF is one of the most admired organizations globally, impacting the lives of children in almost every country of the world. Your work with UNICEF will enable this life-saving and critical work in the most direct way: by raising money that lets us help the most difficult to reach.

UNICEF is at an exciting time as it expands its digital footprint, and we need great professionals to support this challenge. This role is an opportunity to take UNICEF’s fundraising to the next level. Come and help save and nurture children’s lives.

UNICEF is looking for a Digital Data Analyst to work closely with our Global Data and Insights team, which sits within the Individual Giving Unit (IG), Division of Private Fundraising and Partnerships (PFP), to support a digital transformation journey aimed at increasing revenues and making an impact on children’s lives around the world. The Digital Data Analyst will support the IG team remotely, a highly committed and performance-driven team, responsible for overseeing a fundraising program that delivers over one billion dollars of income every year.

The Digital Data Analyst will be responsible for implementing and developing complete and complex data models for donor acquisition and retention that will become a global standard for UNICEF IG team. With those data models as the foundation the Digital Data Analyst will be creating other data products like Dashboards, Machine Learning (ML) models and will ensure that all products are future-proof and AI-ready.

The Digital Data Analyst Is Expected To

  • Act as a subject matter expert in data analytics and GCP/Salesforce data technologies for digital channels.
  • Work with client teams to design and implement modern, scalable data solutions using a range of new and emerging technologies from the Google Cloud Platform and Databricks.
  • Work within an Agile environment.
  • Build and deliver Data and reporting solutions using GCP products and offerings.
  • Build and deliver data models that enable integrated donor feature base which will enable powerful propensity models based on multiple channel and behavioral data point that are easily replicable.

If you would like to know more about this consultancy, please review the complete Terms of Reference here: ToR Digital Data Analyst Consultant.pdf

To qualify as an advocate for every child you will have:

Minimum Requirements

Education: Bachelor’s degree in in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, IT, or any other related field.

Work Experience

  • 5 years of progressively responsible professional work experience in data and analytics

Knowledge/Expertise/Skills

  • Experience in cloud technologies, in particular Google BigQuery is required.
  • Strong SQL knowledge and experience working with relational and non-relational databases is required.
  • Strong communication skills are required, with the ability to convey complex information in a clear and understandable manner.
  • Experience in building analytics dashboards and using data to drive insights for campaign optimization is highly desirable.

Language Requirements

  • Fluency in English is required. Knowledge of other official UN language (Arabic, Chinese, French, Russian or Spanish) is considered as an asset.

Desirables

  • A strong willingness to learn new technologies and stay up to date with trends in the fundraising space
  • Ability to explain marketing solutions and data to non-technical colleagues

For every Child, you demonstrate...

UNICEF’s Core Values of Care, Respect, Integrity, Trust and Accountability and Sustainability (CRITAS) underpin everything we do and how we do it. Get acquainted with Our Values Charter: UNICEF Values

UNICEF promotes and advocates for the protection of the rights of every child, everywhere, in everything it does and is mandated to support the realization of the rights of every child, including those most disadvantaged, and our global workforce must reflect the diversity of those children. The UNICEF family is committed to include everyone, irrespective of their race/ethnicity, disability, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, nationality, socio-economic background, minority, or any other status.

UNICEF encourages applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of gender, nationality, religious or ethnic backgrounds, and from people with disabilities, including neurodivergence. We offer reasonable accommodation for persons with disabilities. throughout the recruitment process. If you require any accommodation, please submit your request through the accessibility email button on the UNICEF Careers webpage Accessibility | UNICEF. Should you be shortlisted, please get in touch with the recruiter directly to share further details, enabling us to make the necessary arrangements in advance.

UNICEF does not hire candidates who are married to children (persons under 18). UNICEF has a zero-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of the United Nations and UNICEF, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and discrimination based on gender, nationality, age, race, sexual orientation, religious or ethnic background or disabilities. UNICEF is committed to promote the protection and safeguarding of all children. All selected candidates will, therefore, undergo rigorous reference and background checks, and will be expected to adhere to these standards and principles. Background checks will include the verification of academic credential(s) and employment history. Selected candidates may be required to provide additional information to conduct a background check, and selected candidates with disabilities may be requested to submit supporting documentation in relation to their disability confidentially.

Qualified candidates are invited to submit the following documents via the online recruitment portal, TMS (Talent Management System):

  • An up-to-date TMS profile and curriculum vitae (CV)
  • Cover letter
  • A separate financial proposal (only acceptable in the format of the linked template): Financial proposal xxx3338.docx

Remarks: If the TOR or financial proposal documents are not visible on certain recruitment platforms, please visit our official page Vacancies | UNICEF Careers.

UNICEF does not charge a processing fee at any stage of its recruitment, selection, and hiring processes (i.e., application stage, interview stage, validation stage, or appointment and training). UNICEF will not ask for applicants’ bank account information.

All UNICEF positions are advertised, and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted and advance to the next stage of the selection process.

Additional information about working for UNICEF can be found here.

Title: Data Analytics Engineer - Python, Cloudera
Description:
Data Analytics Engineer - Python, Cloudera vacancy for our Basel based client in the Banking sector.

Your tasks:




Develop code and implement end-to-end data pipelines with optimal extraction, transformation and loading of data from diverse sources using Spark, SQL and other modern technologies.
Collaborate with business analysts and stakeholders to identify, capture, and analyse requirements, translating them into effective technical solutions.
Contribute to the design and evolution of the overall data architecture, ensuring alignment with modern data management practices.
Maintain and support data pipelines on shared data platforms, while incorporating business rules, security and retention requirements.
Support proof of concepts, stay current with industry standards and assist with the adoption of new technologies in the data platform area.







Your experience/knowledge:




Minimum 5 years of hands-on experience working with modern data technologies, including building data ingestion pipelines for data warehouses or lake/lakehouse architectures.
Proven experience with Python and open-source data technologies such as Hadoop, Spark, Hive, Kafka, or similar, preferably with Cloudera.
Strong background in data modelling, design patterns, and building scalable applications.
Experience within banking/financial institutions, with knowledge of financial instruments, trade lifecycle, or risk management is an advantage.
Language skills: English







Your soft skills:




Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts in simple terms.
Collaborative mindset and capacity to work effectively within multidisciplinary teams.
Strong problem-solving skills and a commitment to continuous learning and improvement.







Location: Basel, Switzerland

Work setting: Hybrid

Sector: Banking

Start: ASAP

Duration: 24 months

Workload: 100%

Ref.Nr.: BH 24332




Take the next step and send us your resume along with a daytime phone number where we can reach you.




Due to Swiss work permit restrictions, we can only consider applications from Swiss nationals, EU citizens, and current work-permit holders for Switzerland.

We welcome applications from individuals of all genders, age groups, sexual orientations, personal expressions, ethnic backgrounds, and religious beliefs. Therefore, there is no requirement to provide gender information or a photo in your application.

As per client requirements, we need information about your marital status, nationality, date of birth, and a valid Swiss work permit. For applicants with disabilities, we are happy to explore potential solutions with our end client.

This job advertisement was prepared with the assistance of AI technology and has undergone human review and approval.

Every application is reviewed personally by our recruiting team - no AI-based screening is used in our selection process.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Data Engineer

Location: Basel, Switzerland

Contract Duration: 3 years, with possible extension

Experience Required: 5+ years. 

Industry Experience: Utilities & Energy (Energie & Wasserversorgung) or Telecommunications or Banking & Financial Services or Information Technology & Services


Role Overview

We are looking for an experienced Data Engineer to support a long-term enterprise project in Basel. This is a 3-year contract role with the possibility of extension.

We are seeking a senior-tier Data Engineer to design, build, and optimize our enterprise-wide data platform. In this role, you will be responsible for breaking down operational data silos and constructing scalable, low-latency ETL/ELT pipelines. Your mission is to establish a unified data architecture that delivers clean, highly structured, and reliable data streams for downstream business intelligence, advanced analytics, and AI initiatives.

Strategic Scope:Architects, deploys, and operates end-to-end automated data ingestion channels, distributed big data pipelines, enterprise analytics platforms, and central repository frameworks.


Core Responsibilities
Pipeline Ingestion Architecture: Analyze raw structured and unstructured source data formats to construct resilient automated ETL/ELT pipelines and real-time streaming ingestion jobs.
Analytical Platform Scaling: Design, scale, and optimize scalable corporate Data Lakes, centralized Enterprise Data Warehouses, and modern Delta Lakehouse architectures.
Governance & Access Enforcements: Enforce data classification standards, programmatic object masking, multi-level role-based access control (RBAC), and automated data quality validation gates.
Lineage & Metadata Mapping: Author, trace, and maintain technical data dictionary mappings, schema evolution profiles, and unified end-to-end data lineage tracking catalogs.
Automation Pipeline Infrastructure: Provision and manage isolated environments using code-driven versioning templates, integrating automated monitoring and security scanning for data flows.

Required Skills & Experience
Minimum of 3 to 5+ years of hands-on experience in Microsoft SQL Server database administration, database engineering, or enterprise database architecture.
Strong experience with SQL Server platform sizing, deployment modelling, resource planning, memory configuration, storage design and performance-oriented database setup.
Good knowledge of data modelling, schema design, indexing strategies, constraints, data types, partitioning and relational database design principles.
Experience designing, implementing and maintaining high-availability and disaster recovery solutions, including failover, mirroring, replication, backup/restore strategies and DR runbooks.
Strong performance tuning experience, including execution plan analysis, stored procedure optimisation, deadlock resolution, nested view tuning and complex query optimisation.
Experience planning and executing database migrations, version upgrades, schema changes, patch deployments and production releases with minimal downtime.
Ability to advise software development teams on database design, query performance, data integrity and scalable application-database interaction patterns.
Good understanding of database security, access control, operational monitoring, incident troubleshooting and enterprise change management practices.
Ability to work independently as well as part of a distributed project team.




Languages : Flawless bilingual/native-level business German communications proficiency (C1/C2 level written and spoken) is required. The candidate must present seamlessly in German during the technical panel interview (Anbieterfachgespräch).
Swiss Compliance : Must hold EU/EFTA nationality or possess an active, valid Swiss permanent residency/work card (C Permit or unrestricted B/G Permit). 




**What We Offer:**




* Opportunity to work on challenging projects and contribute to the growth of our company
* Collaborative and dynamic work environment
* Professional development and growth opportunities
* Competitive salary and benefits package




Recruitment Process – AI Screening with TARA
As part of our recruitment process, candidates may be invited to complete an initial AI-powered screening with TARA, the ALLPS.AI interview agent.
TARA helps us make the hiring process faster, fairer, and more consistent by asking structured, role-relevant questions based on the job requirements. The interview is designed to understand your experience, skills, motivation, and suitability for the role.
The AI screening interview may include:
Questions about your professional background and relevant experience
Role-specific technical or functional questions
Questions about your motivation, availability, and communication skills
The interview can usually be completed online in 15-20 minutes at a time that is convenient for you. Your responses will be reviewed as part of the overall selection process, together with your CV, application details, and any follow-up interviews with the hiring team.




Candidate Experience.:

We use AI to support the recruitment process, but final hiring decisions are made by people. Human recruiters and hiring managers remain involved in reviewing candidates and making selection decisions.
We are committed to a transparent, respectful, and fair candidate experience. Your data will be handled confidentially and in accordance with applicable data protection requirements.
If you are a motivated and experienced Data Engineer looking for a new challenge, please submit your application, including your resume and a cover letter, to our website. We look forward to hearing from you.

Title: Working Student PowerBI Development & Data Analytics 40-60%
Description:
Youtube Video

Together with our customers, we combine the real and digital worlds

With technology, software and services for smart buildings, we transform the everyday for everyone. By combining the real and digital worlds, we empower our customers to reach their decarbonization goals, lower their operating costs and create world-class occupant experiences for their people. It’s not just about improving buildings - it’s about creating efficient, safe, adaptable and sustainable environments, which improve the way the world lives and works.

The digital transformation of buildings is a fast-changing, dynamic sector, and it needs more than just great technology - it needs great people, too. We know a business only thrives if its people are thriving, so at Siemens we put our people first.

Within our buildings business, our diverse global team will support you through your career and challenge you to grow in new and exciting ways, as we transform the world’s infrastructure.

Our shared journey could take you anywhere…where would you like to go?

As a student trainee, intern or trainee, you are an important part of #TeamSiemens. Would you also like to play an active role in influencing current trends and developments and make a positive impact on the lives of millions of people in Switzerland? Then you’ve come to the right place! Apply today for the position of Power BI Developer and Data Analytics Working Student - your team is looking forward to meeting you!

Your New Responsibilities


Closing the Data Gap: Support our monetization initiative via data automation. You will drive insights into risks and opportunities closing the gap between fast data generation and strategic decision/action
Productivity & Cash Flow Analytics: Create deep-dive insights via data analytics regarding team utilization and management, benchmarking these metrics directly against our cash flow performance
Data Quality Assurance: Support GEC Power BI accuracy through rigorous data quality checks, ensuring the absolute reliability, consistency, and transparency of data for the organization
Step-by-Step Introduction: We will introduce you to the commercial steering of our programs. You will get familiar with our commercial operations by gradually taking over foundational tasks in program-level reporting
Risk & Opportunity Management: Over time, you will learn to identify, analyze, and track key commercial risks and opportunities (R&O) across various GEC programs
Commercial Steering: As you grow in the role, you will play an active part in tracking critical commercial topics, preparing variance analyses, and supporting the team in strategic program steering
Automation, Standardization & Collaboration
Process Standardization: Drive execution efficiency and scale by setting up digital agents, automation workflows, and standardized data processes
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner closely with key stakeholders across Finance, Customer Development, Engineering, and Program Execution to understand their unique data challenges and translate them into impactful solutions
Financial Planning & Operations
Forecasting & Planning: Actively support the team in the preparation of monthly forecasts (FC) and the updating of our 5-year strategic plans
Continuous Improvement


Your Talents And Experience


You are currently enrolled pursuing a degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Data Analytics, Business Informatics, or a related quantitative field or University of Applied Sciences in Switzerland
1 to 2 years of experience with Microsoft PowerBI Desktop, or a strong interest and aptitude for learning its functionalities
Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organize, analyze, and disseminate significant amounts of information with attention to detail and accuracy
Basic understanding of software development processes and methodologies
Familiarity with version control systems, preferably GitLab
Proficiency in a programming language commonly used for data manipulation (e.g., Python, SQL, TypeScript) is a plus
You are a motivated and proactive student with excellent problem-solving skills and a dedicated, results-oriented work style
Basic understanding of financial concepts and the underlying logic of financial data (e.g., revenue, cost structures, budgeting, KPIs)
Fluent in English is a must, knowledge of German will be a benefit in this role


Employee Benefits


Mobile working from anywhere within Switzerland
Opportunity to develop an international professional network
Gain experience at one of the largest tech employers in Switzerland
Membership in the Siemens Students Association comprising 200 members


Our corporate culture - your new working environment

The culture at Siemens Switzerland is more than just a working environment - it is at the heart of our day-to-day interactions and the key to our success. Siemens Switzerland offers a future-oriented working environment characterized by innovation, sustainability and personal development. We are characterized by personal responsibility, equal opportunities and diversity, to which every employee can contribute authentically. Flexible working models and remote working are a reality for us and enable our employees to achieve a good work-life balance. Learn more and find out if you are a good match with Siemens by taking our culture quiz!

Frequently asked questions and contact information

Here you will find a collection of frequently asked questions and a way to contact us directly.

I look forward to receiving your application.

Nathalie

Talent Acquisition Partner

Your way to Siemens - How we hire

Information for recruitment agencies: Siemens is not accepting applications from recruitment agencies for this position. Thank you for your understanding.

Title: Data Analyst (Onsite) Materialfluss (M/W/D)*
Description:
Die TGW Logistics Group ist weltweit führend in der Entwicklung und Implementierung hochdynamischer, automatisierter und schlüsselfertiger Logistiklösungen. Mit über 27 Standorten und mehr als 4.500 Mitarbeitenden bieten wir massgeschneiderte Logistiklösungen für Unternehmen aus verschiedenen Branchen.

Möchtest du aktiv zur Verbesserung der Anlagenverfügbarkeit und -performance bei Bell Schweiz AG beitragen? Als Onsite Specialist Engineer IT Materialfluss bist du ein zentraler Bestandteil unseres On-Site-Teams. Du überwachst unsere Systeme kontinuierlich, erkennst Optimierungspotenziale und stellst sicher, dass unsere hochmodernen Logistiklösungen jederzeit effizient und störungsfrei laufen. Gemeinsam mit unserem Team setzt du innovative Lösungen um und gewährleistest unseren Kunden einen erstklassigen Service. Werde Teil unserer innovativen Technologie-Community und helfe uns, die Zukunft der Logistik zu gestalten!

Was man bei uns bewegt:


Spezialist und Ansprechpartner bei Anlagenstörungen für alle materialflussbezogene Fragen
Durchführung regelmässiger Systemarbeiten in Bezug auf Software sowie Instandhaltungsarbeiten an der IT-Infrastruktur
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit dem Lagerleitstand, IT und Steuerungs-Support, 2nd Level Support, Entwicklungsabteilung und dem Kunden
Analyse des Kundensystems für Korrekturmassnahmen und Anpassungen sowie Erstellung von Fehleranalysen
Eigenverantwortliches Beheben von Störungen bzw. Eskalationssteuerung an den Customer Support


Was uns überzeugt:


Abgeschlossene Informatik-Ausbildung oder vergleichbare Berufserfahrung

Title: Data Product Analyst
Description:
Who is Sonar?

Sonar is driving the future of agent-centric software development. As the leader in AI code review and verification, we solve a critical problem: ensuring that software generated by AI-assisted developers or autonomous agents is reliable, secure, and maintainable.

Integrating seamlessly with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Gemini, and Devin, we help over 75% of the Fortune 100 build trusted, reliable, compliant software. Customers who use Sonar are 44% less likely to report an outage due to AI-generated code.

We believe code verification is the critical missing link in the Agent-Centric Development Cycle (AC/DC). Industry giants like Nvidia, ServiceNow, Booking.com, Goldman Sachs, AstraZeneca, and Ford Motor Company count on us to provide independent, explainable, consistent review and governance of their AI-generated code via products like:


SonarQube: The world’s leading AI code review and verification platform.
SonarQube Foundation Agent: Currently topping the leaderboards for agentic software repair.
SonarSweep & Sonar Context Augmentation: Providing the enterprise-grade context and constraints agents need to be truly effective.


Our team operates across global hubs in Austin, Bochum, Dubai, Geneva, London, Singapore, Tokyo, and Washington D.C. We move with a mindset we call CODE:


Committed to our customers and community.
Obsessed with quality.
Deliberate in our decisions.
Effective as one team.


With over $400M in revenue and profitable, fast-paced growth, we are building the backbone of the AI software revolution. If you’re hungry to have an impact, want to build at a fast pace, and ready to work at the forefront of AI, we want to hear from you.

Position Description

We want to make the best decisions possible for our products and to do that, we need to make data-driven decisions. To achieve this goal, we are looking for a talented Data Product Analyst (DPA) to join the product data team.

As a Data Product Analyst (DPA) at Sonar, you are the architect of clarity and the bridge between technical infrastructure and business value. We treat Data as a Product, and you own its entire lifecycle—from dbt SQL modeling and event-based schema governance to high-level executive storytelling.

You will thrive in this role if you enjoy a "full-stack" analytical challenge. You are a proactive project leader who owns the data relationship for your domain. Depending on the need, your impact ranges from empowering users through data literacy to leading complex, end-to-end analyses from initial conception to the final executive summary.

What You Will Do

We believe in using the best tools to stay effective and efficient. You will be expected to master and evolve our data ecosystem:

Data project ownership & delivery


Managing a data project from start to finish using agile method, helping stakeholders to refine their questions into structured data projects, advising stakeholders on what to measure, how to model it, and which tool to use and facilitating stakeholder discussions to bridge the gap between high-level business goals and technical execution.
Deliver end-to-end solutions, from ingestion and modeling to executive-ready visualizations and summaries.


Technical stack


Modeling & Architecture: Build and maintain scalable models using dbt (SQL) and provide critical reviews of event schemas for our event-based platform.
Modern Workflow: Leverage Cursor and Git to accelerate development and ensure code quality and version control.
The Analytics Hub: Own the user experience across Mixpanel, Tableau, Metabase, and Atlan, ensuring they serve as the single source of truth.


Data literacy


Onboarding and empowering users to self-serve, effectively reducing the data team as a bottleneck.
Handling ad-hoc requests ranging from querying specific datasets to providing technical guidance on our tooling landscape.
Participating in Level 1 technical support, including monitoring dbt jobs and Tableau extracts to ensure the reliability of our products.
Project Governance: Use Jira for roadmap visibility and dbt/Confluence/Atlan for high-quality documentation that survives handoffs.


Continuous Platform Improvement

You act as a bridge to Data Engineering. You’ll proactively surface data debt, documentation gaps, and tooling friction to ensure the platform gets simpler and stronger over time. You’ll also ensure high standards for PII handling and security policies are built into every delivery.

Experience And Qualifications


Proven Experience: 3–5+ years in a Data Analyst, Data Product Analyst, or Analytics Engineering role, ideally within a fast-paced environment.
Project Leadership: A track record of taking the lead with stakeholders—successfully translating high-level business ambiguity into structured, actionable data projects. You have experience helping and mentoring other team members.
Technical Mastery: hands-on experience with dbt / SQL (modeling, testing, and documentation).
Hands-on experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, Mixpanel, or similar)
The Modern Workflow: Comfort working in a version-controlled environment (Git) and an interest in leveraging AI-assisted development tools (like Cursor) to drive efficiency.
Data Storytelling: The ability to distill complex findings into executive-ready summaries and visualizations that drive decision-making.
You are fluent in English both written and spoken
Familiarity with data governance practices (PII handling and access control).
Experience in Product Analytics or working directly with Product Managers a nice to have
Familiarity with data engineering concepts (Data Lake, Data Warehouse, ETL / ELT pipeline) and tools (AWS, redshift, Lamdas, Databricks) is a plus


We value diversity, equity, and inclusion

At Sonar, we believe that our diversity is our strength. We are a global company that values and respects different backgrounds, perspectives, and cultures. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment where everyone feels valued and empowered to contribute their best. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and welcome all qualified applicants, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

If you need any accommodation, please reach out to us at [email protected] .

All offers of employment at Sonar are contingent upon the results of a comprehensive background check and reference verification conducted before the start date.

We do not currently support visa candidates in the US.

Applications that are submitted through agencies or third party recruiters will not be considered.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Senior Product Analyst (Growing towards Team Lead)
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Senior Product Analyst (Growing towards Team Lead) based in Switzerland.

You will join a high-impact product analytics environment supporting a global portfolio of large-scale social discovery platforms used by millions of users worldwide. The role combines deep analytical execution with a structured path toward leadership, making it ideal for someone ready to step beyond pure analysis into ownership of cross-functional initiatives. You will work closely with Product, Marketing, and Growth teams to uncover insights that directly influence product direction, monetization, and user engagement. The environment is fast-moving, data-driven, and highly collaborative, with strong emphasis on experimentation, measurable outcomes, and business impact. In addition to hands-on analytics, you will gradually take on coordination responsibilities and stakeholder-facing ownership. This is a growth-oriented role designed to evolve into a Product Analytics Team Lead position over time.

Accountabilities

You will be responsible for driving product and marketing analytics, generating actionable insights, and progressively taking ownership of broader analytical initiatives and leadership responsibilities.


Analyze product, marketing, and business performance to identify key drivers of KPI changes and uncover root causes.
Conduct deep-dive analyses across user behavior, funnels, cohorts, retention, and lifecycle metrics to inform product decisions.
Design, evaluate, and interpret experiments including A/B tests and advanced statistical approaches to validate product hypotheses.
Measure and quantify the impact of product and marketing initiatives on key business outcomes and growth metrics.
Partner closely with Product, Growth, and Marketing teams to support decision-making with data-driven insights.
Support development of marketing analytics frameworks including LTV, CAC, attribution modeling, and funnel optimization.
Contribute to analytics infrastructure improvements and ensure consistency, accuracy, and reliability of core metrics.
Gradually take ownership of cross-functional analytical initiatives and participate in stakeholder coordination and planning.


Requirements

You should bring strong hands-on product analytics expertise, advanced technical skills, and a strong interest in stepping into a leadership track over time.


Strong experience in product analytics within digital or consumer-facing products.
Advanced SQL skills and solid experience working with large datasets and complex data structures.
Deep understanding of product metrics, funnels, retention, cohort analysis, and experimentation frameworks.
Experience in A/B testing methodologies and statistical evaluation of product experiments.
Strong business acumen with the ability to translate data insights into clear, actionable recommendations.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills across technical and non-technical teams.
Ownership mindset with the ability to work independently and drive analytical initiatives end-to-end.
Strong interest in mentoring, leadership development, and progression toward a Team Lead role.
Upper-Intermediate (B2) or higher English proficiency.
Availability to work within GMT+4 to GMT+10 time zones (9:00-18:00 alignment).
Experience in marketplaces, subscription products, SaaS, gaming, dating, or social platforms is a plus.
Exposure to building analytics frameworks, metric systems, or data governance practices is a strong advantage.


Benefits


Fully remote work opportunity across multiple global locations
28 calendar days of annual paid vacation
7 additional wellness days for rest and personal recovery
Up to $5,000 referral bonuses for successful candidate recommendations
50% reimbursement for professional training, courses, and international conferences
Corporate discounts for English language learning programs
Health benefits or up to $1,000 annual reimbursement for medical expenses or insurance
Home office or co-working support with equipment provided or reimbursement options
Internal gamified reward system with redeemable perks and team experiences
Long-term career progression path toward Product Analytics Team Lead role


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Associate Director, Forecasting Data
Description:
Role Overview

The Data Leader – Forecasting will be responsible for building and governing high-quality, AI-ready data foundations to enable forecasting at scale. This role will drive the creation of certified data products and ensure data reliability, consistency, and accessibility across the enterprise.

Key Responsibilities

Data Strategy & Architecture


Define the data strategy for forecasting (demand, supply, inventory, pricing, external signals)
Design scalable data architecture on cloud platforms (e.g., GCP lakehouse)
Standardize data models and semantic layers across regions


Data Product Development


Build and own certified data products (forecast inputs, demand signals, master data)
Define SLAs, quality metrics, and certification standards
Enable self-service consumption for AI models and business users


Data Governance & Quality


Establish governance frameworks (ownership, lineage, cataloging, access control)
Ensure data quality, consistency, and completeness across multiple ERPs
Drive data readiness for AI use cases


Integration & Platform Enablement


Lead data ingestion and integration from internal systems and external sources
Collaborate with platform teams on pipelines, transformations, and storage
Ensure scalability and performance of data pipelines


Stakeholder Collaboration


Partner with AI teams, business stakeholders, and IT teams
Act as the bridge between business data needs and technical implementation
Drive data literacy and adoption across teams


Key Skills & Qualifications


10–15 years in data engineering, data architecture, or data platforms
Strong experience with cloud data platforms (GCP, Snowflake, Databricks)
Expertise in data modeling, ETL/ELT, and data governance
Experience working in multi-ERP, complex enterprise environments
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills


Success Metrics


Number of certified data products created
Data quality scores (accuracy, completeness, timeliness)
Time to enable new AI use cases
Reduction in data-related issues/escalations


Pay Range

CHF140,000.00 - CHF280,000.00

Title: IT & Market Data Procurement Analyst
Description:
Established in 2004, Mercuria is one of the leading integrated energy and commodity trading companies in the world. We bring energy markets together to support the needs of today by trading, structuring finance, and investing into strategic assets, while generating more than $110 billion in turnover.




Our operations span over 50 countries on 5 continents, including all the major energy hubs. We trade physical oil, energy products, environmental products and other commodities from Geneva, London, Singapore, Shanghai, Beijing, Dubai, Houston, Calgary and Greenwich (CT).




We are committed to advancing the transition to a more sustainable, affordable and reliable energy system for tomorrow. Over 50% of our assets are in low carbon & energy transition sectors - providing a strong platform to trade these new markets and support decarbonization.




In 2023, we established Silvania, a $500 million fund, investing into restoration and protection of nature and biodiversity globally and in support of the Paris Agreement goals & UN 30x30 biodiversity initiative. This new asset class supports nature protection and provides sustainable financial returns.










The role




This role sits within the CIO Office in Geneva and is responsible for end-to-end procurement, commercial management, and financial governance across Mercuria’s global technology estate, with a particular focus on IT services, software, and market data.

The position combines hands-on operational execution with commercial ownership and transformation, supporting IT leadership and senior business stakeholders to optimise technology spend, manage vendor relationships, and professionalise procurement processes across a fast-paced commodity trading environment.

The role actively promotes the adoption of data, automation, and AI-enabled capabilities within procurement activities

The role has global scope, high visibility, and regular exposure to senior management.

Title: Contrôleur de gestion junior & Data analyst (h/f/x)
Description:
Vos missions

En qualité de Contrôleur de gestion junior & Data analyst (h/f/x), vous assurez le suivi des dépenses par centre de frais,
Vous analysez et commentez les écarts du compte de résultat (financier et analytique),
Vous soutenez l’établissement du budget par centre de frais,
Vous participez aux bouclements mensuels ainsi qu’à l’élaboration de plans futurs,
Vous développez des KPI et des rapports / outils BI de performance en collaboration avec les responsables fonctionnels de l’entreprise,
Vous réalisez des analyses spécifiques et pilotez les mesures d’optimisation,


Votre profil

Vous êtes titulaire d’un Bachelor ou d’un Master en économie d’entreprise, finance ou dans un domaine similaire,
Vous bénéficiez d’au minimum 2 à 3 ans d’expérience dans le controlling ou l’analyse de données, idéalement dans un environnement industriel,
Vous maîtrisez le français et l’anglais et possédez de bonnes connaissances en allemand,
Vous maîtrisez la suite MS Office et disposez de bonnes connaissances d’un ERP,
Vous êtes une personne autonome, flexible et dotée de bonnes aptitudes à travailler en équipe,
Vous vous distinguez par vos compétences analytiques, vos aptitudes en communication ainsi que votre capacité à vous imposer.


Notre offre
Pouvons-nous vous motiver à endosser un rôle clé dans une entreprise familiale, dynamique et indépendante ? Nous vous encourageons à partager et à développer vos idées et vous offrons un emploi sûr, des conditions de travail modernes et conciliables avec la vie familiale telles que des horaires de travail flexibles, la possibilité de travailler en Home office, un restaurant d'entreprise, des perspectives de formation continue et de nombreux autres avantages. Le lieu de travail se situe à Bulle, dans la belle région de la Gruyère. Planifiez avec nous votre développement personnel et professionnel.
Afin de garantir une procédure de candidature équitable, nous n’acceptons pas les candidatures par courrier postal ou électronique.
Ce poste est soumis à une procédure de vérification des antécédents.
Avons-nous suscité votre intérêt ? Si c’est le cas, nous serions ravis de recevoir votre candidature en ligne. Si vous avez des questions, veuillez contacter Nadege MALAN.
One Passion. Many Opportunities.

Une passion commune. Tant d’opportunités.

Liebherr Machines Bulle SA est le centre de compétences des moteurs à combustion (diesel et gaz) et composants hydrauliques. Title: GLP Auditor
Description:
Job Title: GLP Auditor

Location: Basel

Contract: Permanent or Temporary




About us

Adryan is an internationally operating services group with offices in The Netherlands and Switzerland. We specialize in providing a full range of services for the life sciences industry, focusing on Project Management, Technical Operations, CQV, and Compliance. Our teams work with top clients in the life sciences sector, ensuring the highest quality and commitment.




Position Summary

We are seeking a quality-oriented GLP Auditor / Data Reviewer (m/f/d). In this role, you will be responsible for reviewing and verifying scientific raw data and paper-based study documentation in accordance with Good Laboratory Practice (GLP).




This role serves as a central interface between the operational laboratory and study departments and Quality Assurance. You will ensure that all data is documented in a complete, consistent, traceable, and regulatory-compliant manner before it is incorporated into reports and regulatory submissions.




Main Responsibilities

Reviewing paper-based study documentation, raw data, laboratory records, and related documents for completeness, consistency, and GLP compliance
Reconciling data from internal databases with the corresponding original documents (paper records)
Identifying, documenting, and tracking deviations, discrepancies, or missing information
Conducting data reviews in accordance with internal QA processes and GLP requirements
Assisting with internal audits and inspections
Creating and maintaining audit trail documentation, review reports, and quality records
Working closely with study directors, scientists, and operational teams to resolve data-related issues
Ensuring data integrity




Requirements:

Experience on Swiss Ordinance on Good Laboratory Practice of 18 May 2005 and the relevant OECD GLP guidelines
QC/QA experience is also considered
Demonstrated proficiency in GLP audit procedures and documentation.
Knowledge of routine analytical methodologies, with an emphasis on chromatography
Knowledge of computerized system validation
Good interpersonal skills with demonstrable tact and diplomacy
Good computer skills including Microsoft Word and Excel. Knowledge of MS-Access and SQL would be a plus
With your openness and understanding of mutuality, your active communication and continuous value assessment you foster and encourage our team mentality. You communicate and work effectively with scientific staff at all levels within the Testing Facility and you see yourself as a creative problem solver.
Good oral and written communication skills in German and English

Title: Data Analyst – Cloud Migration & BigQuery
Description:
Descrizione Del Lavoro

Our organization is launching a strategic initiative to migrate its data infrastructure from an on-premise Oracle Database environment to Google Cloud Platform (GCP). The project involves consolidating legacy data warehouses, re-architecting ETL/ELT pipelines, and adopting cloud-native services such as BigQuery, Cloud Storage, and Dataflow.

The selected Data Analyst will be a key contributor across every phase of the migration lifecycle: from as-is data analysis of source systems, through post-migration validation, to supporting business teams in their transition to new cloud-based analytics tools

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Mapping


Inventory and document Oracle source data (schemas, tables, stored procedures, views)
Perform data profiling to identify anomalies, duplicates, and inconsistencies
Define logical data mappings from Oracle to BigQuery for each data domain
Collaborate with data engineers on the definition of transformation rules


Validation & Quality Assurance


Design and implement data reconciliation tests for pre- and post-migration phases
Develop SQL / BigQuery SQL queries to compare source and target datasets
Produce data quality reports covering completeness, accuracy, and consistency metrics
Log and track data anomalies using issue-tracking tools
Strong data modeling skills, including conceptual, logical, and physical design, with hands-on experience in normalization and dimensional modeling (star/snowflake schema).﻿


Migration Support ﻿


Actively participate in sprint planning and stand-ups with technical and business teams
Support the migration of legacy reports into GCP-native tools (Looker, Looker Studio)
Contribute to the definition of data governance standards and cloud best practices
Document data transformation processes for knowledge transfer purposes


Personal Profile & Soft Skills﻿


Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail
Ability to communicate complex data findings to non-technical stakeholders
Operational autonomy and a proactive approach to problem-solving
Good time management in multi-task and project-driven environments
Team player: comfortable working in cross-functional IT/Business teams
Flexibility and adaptability in contexts of technological change
Language: Italian Native or Bilingual
Language: English (written and spoken, minimum B2 level)


Education


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field
Specialization courses in data engineering, cloud computing, or analytics are a plus

Title: Data Engineer - Digital Assets
Description:
IMC has a strong presence in traditional Finance and has been expanding into Crypto over the last years. Through its overseas affiliates, IMC provides liquidity and trades on many cryptocurrency exchanges and DeFi protocols. You can expect a dynamic, fast-paced environment that delivers a steady stream of challenges and opportunities, with one of the most exciting aspects of the job being our quick feedback loop in the research, development and deployment of strategies.

As a Data Engineer, you‘ll be joining our core engineering team. You will contribute to our proprietary low-latency trading system, acquire market and trading data, implement ETL processes and collaborate with traders and researchers on improving the trading results.

Your Core Responsibilities


Collaborate on developing market and trading data pipeline systems, both in batch mode and streaming mode.
Extend data pipelines to multiple exchanges/platforms and across different areas of application.
Work closely together with other engineers and researchers, providing efficient data solutions for our research and trading operations.


Your Skills And Experience


Masters in Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics or a related technical field. Industry experience in big data would be a big plus.
Skills in data pipeline technologies such as Clickhouse, Flink and Kafka. Experience with Kubernetes is a plus.
Proficient programming experience in Java/Scala & Python, C++ being a plus.
You are absolutely fluent in English and enjoy working in an international team.
You are eager to design, develop, test, deploy, maintain and improve software.


Location and Office Culture:


IMC office in Zug, Switzerland
Note that this is an onsite role, with some but limited flexibility for working from home
You can expect a dynamic, fast-paced environment that delivers a steady stream of challenges and opportunities
One of the most exciting aspects of the job is our quick feedback loop in the research, development and deployment of strategies
We give people lots of responsibility and the support they need to make an impact from day one
We promise you a flat structure and an open, collaborative culture that stimulates the sharing of ideas, knowledge and technology between our teams and offices


About Us

IMC is a global trading firm powered by a cutting-edge research environment and a world-class technology backbone. Since 1989, we’ve been a stabilizing force in financial markets, providing essential liquidity upon which market participants depend. Across our offices in the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, and India, our talented quant researchers, engineers, traders, and business operations professionals are united by our uniquely collaborative, high-performance culture, and our commitment to giving back. From entering dynamic new markets to embracing disruptive technologies, and from developing an innovative research environment to diversifying our trading strategies, we dare to continuously innovate and collaborate to succeed.

Title: Real-World Evidence (RWE) Analyst / Engineer
Description:
Your mission

We are looking for a motivated and versatile RWE Analyst / Engineer to join our growing team. This role combines technical data work with analytical thinking and exposure to high-impact projects in the pharmaceutical industry.

You will work across a variety of projects, disease areas, and data sources, contributing to the generation and interpretation of real-world evidence in Europe and beyond. You will collaborate closely with physicians, medical registries, and leading pharmaceutical companies to translate complex data into actionable insights that inform clinical and regulatory decision-making.

Your responsibilities:


Build and maintain data pipelines and support data engineering tasks
Conduct statistical analyses on real-world healthcare data
Contribute or lead to the interpretation of results and generation of insights
Support or lead the development of Statistical Analysis Plans (SAPs)
Collaborate closely with physicians, clinical experts, and medical registries (e.g., in neurology, rheumatology, gastroenterology, dermatology)
Work with cross-functional teams across multiple projects


Your profile

Required


Master’s degree in a technical or quantitative field (e.g., data science, pharmaco-epidemiology, statistics, engineering, computer science)
Strong programming/engineering skills (R preferred; general purpose programming language such as Python acceptable)
Solid understanding of data analysis and statistics
Fluency in English and German
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word)
High level of flexibility and a service-oriented mindset


Nice to Have


PhD and/or relevant work experience
Research expertise
Experience in the pharmaceutical industry, ideally in a regulatory context
Knowledge of advanced statistical modeling (e.g., causal inference methods)


Why us?


Unique data access: Work with exclusive real-world datasets and medical registries across Europe
Science meets consulting: Operate at the intersection of rigorous analytics and strategic thinking, combining a scientific mindset with a client-oriented, consulting approach
High-impact projects: Collaborate with leading global pharmaceutical companies on strategically relevant evidence generation
Entrepreneurial culture: Flat hierarchies, fast decision-making, and direct exposure to clients and senior experts
Flexibility: Remote-friendly setup with a high degree of autonomy and trust
Innovation-driven environment: Leverage real-world evidence to help shape and develop next-generation digital health solutions


About Us

At Rewoso, we specialize in the generation of high-quality real-world evidence to support clinical, regulatory, and strategic decision-making in healthcare.

On a day-to-day basis, we work with real-world data from European medical registries—to design & develop statistical approaches, and translate data into actionable insights in close collaboration with physicians and pharmaceutical partners.

Building on this experience, we go beyond evidence generation: we leverage our deep understanding of real-world clinical practice and data to develop innovative digital health solutions that aim to improve patient outcomes.

Learn more at: www.rewoso.com
